/

Gemini Enterprise for                           HR & PeopleOps                                Agentic Transformation

TRANSFORMATION USE CASES FIELD KIT

User Personas • Process Domains • Processes • Process Activities • Day-in-the-Life Scenarios

Process Domain → Process → Agentic Use Cases

Process Domain Coverage

12 Personas, 10 Domains

Process Coverage

36 Processes

Agentic Use Cases

82 Use Cases

DRAFT Last UpdatedMar 31, 2026

AUTHOR - Shanky RamPrincipal Architect, Google Cloud APAC — Strategic Industries & Partnerships

PITCH DECK - PITCH - Gemini Enterprise for Transforming HR & People Operations

Document Purpose

This Field Kit is a three-document deliverable set designed to take an organization from process understanding through agentic opportunity identification to implementation-ready discovery — in a single, connected arc.

Chapter 1 — HR Process Domain Architecture establishes the foundational blueprint. It defines the user personas who operate within HR and PeopleOps, catalogs process domains containing multiple end-to-end processes, and documents the complete activity sequence, unstructured knowledge sources, and structured data products for each. A RACI-style persona-to-domain alignment matrix ensures clear accountability, and day-in-the-life scenarios for all personas ground the architecture in the reality of daily work — making it possible to see exactly where time is spent, what information is consumed, and where friction lives. This chapter is deliberately platform-agnostic. It describes the work as it exists today, independent of any technology solution, so that it can serve as a durable reference even as tooling evolves.

Chapter 2 — HR / PeopleOps Systems Landscape & Process Transformation Roadmap presents and catalogs the complete HR technology ecosystem that agents can interact with across all ten process domains. For each system category, it shows typical HR systems in the market, the current role the system plays in HR operations, and how that role changes in the transformed state.

This section refers to “Appendix A - HR & PeopleOps Process Transformation Blueprint (AS IS vs TO BE)” that presents a side-by-side comparison of how every HR process transforms from its current state to an AI-augmented future state with Gemini Enterprise agents. For each process, every activity step is decomposed into: the as-is state (what happens today), the to-be state (how named agents transform the step), expected qualitative impact (what changes in practice), and key KPI metrics (how to measure success without prescribing specific numeric targets, which require organization-specific baselining).

It also presents a multi phase transformation approach, organized by business value and organizational readiness rather than by technology layer. Each phase builds on the prior: Phase 1 establishes the agent platform and delivers quick wins, Phase 2 extends coverage and deepens integration, Phase 3 introduces predictive and analytical capabilities, and Phase 4 shifts to continuous optimization.

Chapter 3 — HR & PeopleOps Agentic Use Case Map takes every process from Chapter 1 and decomposes it into agent-addressable segments. 82 purpose-built agents are identified, each mapped to the specific activities within a process that it would perform. Agents are classified into four implementation layer archetypes — out-of-the-box Gemini Enterprise features, agents built via Agent Designer with third-party connectors, custom agents on the ADK runtime with Gemini Enterprise as the interaction layer, and data agents operating against the analytics tier.

Chapter 4 — HR & PeopleOps Agentic Use Case Deep Dive converts the use case map into an actionable transformation roadmaps and agentic process redesign framework.

Where a single process requires multiple agents working on different portions of the activity sequence, the multi-agent orchestration pattern is documented. A layer distribution analysis by domain and a four-phase implementation sequencing recommendation close the chapter, giving leadership a clear view of where quick wins sit versus where engineering investment is required.

For each of the 82 agents a deep dive of the agent functionality is detailed. The  key discovery questions probe the critical unknowns that must be resolved before implementation: current-state maturity, data and system availability, integration feasibility, governance and access control requirements, and success measurement criteria. The questions are presented in a single reference table organized by process domain, designed to be projected in stakeholder sessions or printed by domain for focused working groups and collectively represent the complete discovery scope for the agentic transformation program in HR & People Operations.

Chapter 1 — HR Process Domain Architecture

1.1 User Personas in HR & PeopleOps

The HR & PeopleOps function is served by twelve distinct user personas, each with unique responsibilities, decision-making authority, information needs, and system interactions. These personas span from executive strategy (CHRO) through operational execution (Payroll Specialist, Recruiter) to enabling functions (People Analytics, HRIS Analyst).

This section defines each persona and maps their primary domain responsibilities, establishing the foundation for process ownership and day-in-the-life scenarios that follow.

Role / Persona

Description

Key Focus Areas

Primary Process Domains

Chief Human Resources Officer (CHRO)

Executive accountable for enterprise-wide people strategy, workforce planning, culture, and board-level HR governance.

Strategy, M&A people integration, executive compensation, DEI, ESG workforce metrics

Workforce Planning & Organizational Design; Performance & Talent Management; Compensation, Benefits & Total Rewards; Employee Engagement & Culture; DEI & Belonging

VP / Director of Talent Acquisition

Leads recruiting strategy across all business units; owns employer branding, sourcing channels, and hiring velocity.

Requisition pipeline, offer competitiveness, time-to-fill, candidate experience, diversity hiring

Talent Acquisition & Onboarding; DEI & Belonging

HR Business Partner (HRBP)

Embedded strategic advisor to a business unit or function; translates business objectives into people interventions.

Org design, succession planning, engagement action plans, change management, manager coaching

Workforce Planning & Organizational Design; Performance & Talent Management; Employee Relations & Compliance; Employee Engagement & Culture; People Analytics & HR Technology

Compensation & Benefits Manager

Designs and administers total rewards philosophy including salary bands, incentive plans, equity programs, and benefits.

Pay equity audits, benchmarking, benefits enrollment, regulatory compliance (FLSA, ACA)

Compensation, Benefits & Total Rewards

HR Operations / People Ops Manager

Runs the operational backbone: HRIS administration, employee data integrity, shared-service delivery, and process optimization.

Employee lifecycle transactions, data quality, SLA management, tier-1/2 query resolution

HR Operations & Shared Services

Learning & Development (L&D) Manager

Owns capability-building strategy, training catalog, leadership development, and learning technology stack.

Skills-gap analysis, content curation, LMS/LXP management, ROI measurement, compliance training

Learning & Development

Employee Relations Specialist

Manages grievances, investigations, disciplinary actions, workplace accommodations, and policy interpretation.

Case management, legal risk mitigation, documentation standards, mediation, policy updates

Employee Relations & Compliance

DEI Program Manager

Drives diversity, equity, and inclusion initiatives; tracks representation metrics and fosters belonging.

ERG management, inclusive hiring practices, pay-gap reporting, cultural programming

DEI & Belonging

People Analytics Analyst

Transforms workforce data into insights; builds dashboards, predictive models, and ad-hoc analyses.

Attrition modeling, engagement drivers, headcount forecasting, survey analytics, HR data governance

People Analytics & HR Technology

Payroll & Compliance Specialist

Ensures accurate, timely payroll processing and regulatory adherence across jurisdictions.

Payroll runs, tax filings, labor-law compliance, audit readiness, statutory reporting

HR Operations & Shared Services; Employee Relations & Compliance

Recruiter / Talent Acquisition Partner

Executes end-to-end hiring for assigned roles: sourcing, screening, interviewing, and closing candidates.

Boolean sourcing, interview coordination, ATS management, offer negotiation, candidate nurturing

Talent Acquisition & Onboarding

HRIS / HR Technology Analyst

Configures, maintains, and optimizes the HR technology ecosystem including HRIS, ATS, and integrations.

System administration, data migrations, workflow automation, vendor management, reporting

People Analytics & HR Technology; HR Operations & Shared Services

1.2 Key Process Domains

HR & PeopleOps management is organized into ten interconnected process domains. Each domain encompasses multiple end-to-end processes that collectively deliver the employee lifecycle and organizational capability-building functions.

The table below provides a summary view with the primary personas responsible for each domain and the constituent processes.

#

Process Domain

Primary Personas

Constituent Processes

1

Workforce Planning & Organizational Design

CHRO, HRBP

Strategic Workforce Planning; Organization Design & Restructuring; Job Architecture & Role Management

2

Talent Acquisition & Onboarding

VP TA, Recruiter

Requisition & Approval; Sourcing & Candidate Pipeline; Interview & Selection; Offer & Pre-boarding; Onboarding & Integration

3

Performance & Talent Management

HRBP, CHRO

Goal Setting & OKR Alignment; Continuous Feedback & Check-ins; Annual/Semi-Annual Performance Review; Succession Planning; High-Potential (HiPo) Program Management

4

Compensation, Benefits & Total Rewards

Comp & Ben Mgr, CHRO

Compensation Benchmarking & Band Design; Annual Compensation Review (Merit & Promotion); Benefits Administration; Equity & Long-Term Incentive Management

5

Learning & Development

L&D Mgr

Training Needs Analysis; Learning Content Development & Curation; Compliance & Mandatory Training; Leadership Development Programs

6

Employee Relations & Compliance

ER Specialist, Payroll Specialist

Grievance & Complaint Management; Disciplinary Action & Performance Improvement; Policy Development & Management; Workplace Accommodations & Leave Management

7

Employee Engagement & Culture

HRBP, CHRO, DEI Mgr

Employee Engagement Surveys; Recognition & Rewards Programs; Internal Communications & Culture Initiatives

8

HR Operations & Shared Services

HR Ops Mgr, Payroll Specialist, HRIS Analyst

Employee Data Management (HRIS); HR Service Delivery (Tier 0/1/2); Payroll Processing; Offboarding & Exit Management

9

DEI & Belonging

DEI Mgr, CHRO

DEI Strategy & Metrics; ERG (Employee Resource Group) Management

10

People Analytics & HR Technology

People Analytics, HRIS Analyst

Workforce Analytics & Reporting; HR Technology Governance

1.3 A Typical Day in the Life of HR Personas

This section presents a comprehensive day-in-the-life scenario for each of the twelve user personas defined in Section 1.1. Each scenario illustrates the activities, decisions, and process touchpoints that the persona navigates during a typical workday. For every time-block, the specific HR processes engaged are cross-referenced to Section 1.4, creating a traceable link between daily work patterns and the process architecture. These scenarios serve as the primary input for Chapter 2, where each activity will be mapped to agentic automation opportunities enabled by Gemini Enterprise.

P01: Chief Human Resources Officer (CHRO)

The CHRO operates at the intersection of business strategy and people strategy, spending the day navigating between board-level governance, cross-functional leadership, and the orchestration of enterprise-wide HR initiatives.

Time

Activity

Processes Engaged

7:00 AM

Review overnight workforce alerts: attrition spikes in engineering Bangalore, union negotiation update from EU counsel, DEI dashboard refresh for board deck.

Workforce Analytics & Reporting, DEI Strategy & Metrics

8:00 AM

Executive Committee meeting: present workforce plan scenario models for FY27, discuss AI-driven productivity projections, approve org restructuring proposal for the commercial division.

Strategic Workforce Planning, Organization Design & Restructuring

9:30 AM

1:1 with VP Talent Acquisition: review Q1 hiring velocity, employer brand campaign results, executive search updates for CTO role.

Sourcing & Candidate Pipeline, Requisition & Approval

10:30 AM

Compensation Committee prep: finalize executive compensation benchmarking analysis, equity refresh recommendations, pay equity audit findings.

Compensation Benchmarking & Band Design, Equity & LTI Management

12:00 PM

Lunch with HRBP leaders: discuss engagement survey action plan progress, manager effectiveness initiative, upcoming RIF communication plan.

Employee Engagement Surveys, Internal Communications & Culture Initiatives

1:30 PM

Review People Analytics quarterly insights deck: attrition prediction model accuracy, flight-risk dashboard for top talent, workforce cost projections.

Workforce Analytics & Reporting, Succession Planning

3:00 PM

M&A integration call: Day-60 people integration status for recent acquisition, culture alignment assessment, retention bonus effectiveness.

Organization Design & Restructuring, Compensation Benchmarking & Band Design

4:30 PM

Sign-off on updated remote work policy; review employee relations escalation summary; approve L&D budget reallocation for AI upskilling initiative.

Policy Development & Management, Leadership Development Programs

P02: VP / Director of Talent Acquisition

The VP of Talent Acquisition balances strategic talent planning with operational recruiting oversight, ensuring the organization attracts top talent while building sustainable hiring capabilities.

Time

Activity

Processes Engaged

7:30 AM

Review weekly recruiting dashboard: open requisition aging, pipeline conversion rates by stage, offer acceptance trends, diversity funnel metrics.

Workforce Analytics & Reporting, Sourcing & Candidate Pipeline

8:30 AM

Recruiter team standup: discuss priority requisitions, candidate pipeline blockers, interview scheduling bottlenecks, and upcoming career fair logistics.

Requisition & Approval, Interview & Selection

9:30 AM

Employer branding strategy session with marketing: review candidate persona research, career site analytics, social media campaign performance, Glassdoor response plan.

Sourcing & Candidate Pipeline, Internal Communications & Culture Initiatives

10:30 AM

Executive search debrief with retained firm: long-list review for VP Engineering role, assess cultural fit criteria, align on interview process.

Interview & Selection, Offer & Pre-boarding

12:00 PM

Lunch & learn with hiring manager cohort: train on structured interviewing, unconscious bias in selection, and scorecard calibration best practices.

Interview & Selection, DEI Strategy & Metrics

1:30 PM

Vendor review: evaluate new AI sourcing tool demo, assess ATS integration capabilities, review contract terms for campus recruiting platform.

HR Technology Governance, Sourcing & Candidate Pipeline

3:00 PM

University relations planning: finalize target school list, design intern-to-FTE conversion program, review early-career competency framework.

Sourcing & Candidate Pipeline, Job Architecture & Role Management

4:30 PM

Review weekly offer approvals queue; approve salary exceptions with compensation team; sign off on Q2 headcount forecast with finance.

Offer & Pre-boarding, Strategic Workforce Planning

P03: HR Business Partner (HRBP)

The HRBP operates as a strategic advisor embedded within business units, translating organizational priorities into actionable people interventions while managing day-to-day HR advisory responsibilities.

Time

Activity

Processes Engaged

8:00 AM

Check HR service portal for escalated employee queries; review overnight case updates in ER system; scan engagement pulse survey results for assigned BU.

HR Service Delivery (Tier 0/1/2), Employee Engagement Surveys

9:00 AM

Weekly sync with BU head: discuss open headcount, team performance concerns, org design options for new product line, upcoming promotion calibration.

Strategic Workforce Planning, Organization Design & Restructuring

10:00 AM

Coach a people manager on handling a performance improvement plan conversation; draft PIP documentation; align with ER specialist on approach.

Disciplinary Action & Performance Improvement, Continuous Feedback & Check-ins

11:00 AM

Facilitate succession planning session: review 9-box placements for director-level roles, identify development actions, update succession charts.

Succession Planning, High-Potential (HiPo) Program Management

12:30 PM

Work with Compensation on off-cycle salary adjustment requests; prepare retention counter-offer analysis for a flight-risk senior engineer.

Annual Compensation Review (Merit & Promotion), Compensation Benchmarking & Band Design

2:00 PM

Lead change management workshop for team restructuring; create stakeholder communication plan; prepare FAQ document for affected employees.

Organization Design & Restructuring, Internal Communications & Culture Initiatives

3:30 PM

Analyze BU-level attrition data; prepare insights memo connecting exit interview themes to engagement scores; draft recommendations for leadership.

Workforce Analytics & Reporting, Offboarding & Exit Management

5:00 PM

Update workforce planning tracker; respond to employee policy clarification emails; prepare for tomorrow’s calibration session.

Strategic Workforce Planning, Annual/Semi-Annual Performance Review

P04: Compensation & Benefits Manager

The Compensation & Benefits Manager navigates a data-intensive day balancing market competitiveness analysis with internal equity, regulatory compliance, and employee-facing benefits programs.

Time

Activity

Processes Engaged

8:00 AM

Review overnight benefits enrollment exception reports; check vendor data feeds for claims processing accuracy; scan regulatory update digest for new state mandates.

Benefits Administration, Policy Development & Management

9:00 AM

Market benchmarking deep-dive: analyze Mercer and Radford survey data for software engineering roles; update compensation bands for three job families flagged as below-market.

Compensation Benchmarking & Band Design, Job Architecture & Role Management

10:00 AM

HRBP consultation: review five off-cycle salary adjustment requests, assess against compa-ratio guidelines, model budget impact, prepare recommendations.

Annual Compensation Review (Merit & Promotion)

11:00 AM

Open enrollment planning meeting: review benefits vendor renewal terms, analyze utilization data, design communication campaign timeline, coordinate with HR Ops on system configuration.

Benefits Administration, Internal Communications & Culture Initiatives

12:30 PM

Pay equity analysis working session with People Analytics: review regression model outputs, identify statistically significant gaps, estimate remediation costs.

Compensation Benchmarking & Band Design, Workforce Analytics & Reporting

2:00 PM

Equity compensation committee prep: model RSU refresh grant scenarios for retention, calculate dilution impact, prepare vesting acceleration analysis for M&A scenario.

Equity & Long-Term Incentive Management

3:30 PM

Review offer packages for three executive hires with VP TA; ensure alignment with compensation philosophy and internal equity; model sign-on bonus amortization.

Offer & Pre-boarding, Compensation Benchmarking & Band Design

4:30 PM

Update total rewards statement template for annual distribution; finalize variable pay plan documentation for legal review; respond to employee benefits queries.

Benefits Administration, HR Service Delivery (Tier 0/1/2)

P05: HR Operations / People Ops Manager

The HR Operations Manager runs the operational engine of the HR function, ensuring data integrity, process efficiency, and seamless service delivery across the employee lifecycle.

Time

Activity

Processes Engaged

7:30 AM

Monitor HRIS system health dashboards; review data quality exception reports; check pending integration sync errors between HRIS and payroll.

Employee Data Management (HRIS)

8:30 AM

Process morning batch of employee lifecycle transactions: new hires, promotions, transfers, compensation changes, terminations.

Employee Data Management (HRIS), Offboarding & Exit Management

9:30 AM

Review HR service desk metrics: case backlog, SLA breaches, top query categories; assign and prioritize escalated cases to team.

HR Service Delivery (Tier 0/1/2)

10:30 AM

Lead weekly ops standup: discuss process bottlenecks, upcoming payroll deadlines, open enrollment preparation status, system maintenance schedule.

Payroll Processing, Benefits Administration

11:30 AM

Work with HRIS analyst on benefits enrollment workflow configuration for upcoming open enrollment; test system changes in sandbox.

Benefits Administration, Employee Data Management (HRIS)

1:00 PM

Coordinate with IT on employee access provisioning/deprovisioning audits; review separation of duties compliance for HRIS admin access.

Employee Data Management (HRIS), Offboarding & Exit Management

2:30 PM

Validate payroll input file: verify new hire data, check garnishment orders, confirm overtime calculations, resolve discrepancies with timekeeping.

Payroll Processing

4:00 PM

Prepare monthly HR operations dashboard: transaction volumes, error rates, SLA performance, employee satisfaction with HR services; onboarding completion report.

HR Service Delivery (Tier 0/1/2), Onboarding & Integration

P06: Learning & Development (L&D) Manager

The L&D Manager spends the day designing, curating, and measuring learning experiences that build organizational capability, from compliance mandates to leadership development and technical upskilling.

Time

Activity

Processes Engaged

8:00 AM

Review LMS dashboard: course completion rates, overdue compliance training flags, learner satisfaction scores from last week’s programs.

Compliance & Mandatory Training, Learning Content Development & Curation

9:00 AM

Training needs analysis session with engineering HRBP: review skills-gap assessment results, identify critical capabilities for cloud migration program, prioritize Q2 learning investments.

Training Needs Analysis

10:00 AM

Content development sprint: review instructional designer’s storyboard for new cybersecurity awareness module; provide feedback on microlearning format and assessment questions.

Learning Content Development & Curation

11:00 AM

Leadership development program design: finalize cohort 5 curriculum, confirm executive coach assignments, prepare pre-program 360 assessment launch.

Leadership Development Programs, High-Potential (HiPo) Program Management

12:30 PM

Vendor review with LinkedIn Learning account manager: analyze utilization data, review content catalog relevance, negotiate enterprise license renewal terms.

Learning Content Development & Curation, HR Technology Governance

2:00 PM

Compliance training escalation: 47 employees overdue on annual harassment prevention training; prepare escalation emails to managers; coordinate with ER on consequence framework.

Compliance & Mandatory Training, Grievance & Complaint Management

3:00 PM

Onboarding program optimization: review new-hire feedback on orientation experience, redesign Day-1 schedule, develop role-specific learning paths for top 5 job families.

Onboarding & Integration, Training Needs Analysis

4:30 PM

Prepare quarterly L&D impact report: training hours delivered, competency assessment improvements, Kirkpatrick Level 3 behavior change data, budget vs. actuals.

Workforce Analytics & Reporting, Training Needs Analysis

P07: Employee Relations Specialist

The Employee Relations Specialist navigates sensitive, high-stakes situations requiring legal knowledge, empathetic communication, and meticulous documentation throughout the day.

Time

Activity

Processes Engaged

8:00 AM

Review overnight ethics hotline submissions: two new anonymous complaints received; categorize by type (harassment, policy violation), assess initial severity, assign case IDs.

Grievance & Complaint Management

9:00 AM

Investigation interview: conduct witness interview for ongoing workplace harassment case; document testimony using structured interview protocol; maintain chain-of-evidence standards.

Grievance & Complaint Management

10:30 AM

PIP review meeting with HRBP: assess progress for two employees on performance improvement plans; one trending positively (extend support), one at risk (prepare documentation for potential termination).

Disciplinary Action & Performance Improvement

11:30 AM

Policy consultation: manager requests guidance on remote work accommodation for employee with documented medical condition; initiate ADA interactive process; coordinate with legal on obligations.

Workplace Accommodations & Leave Management, Policy Development & Management

1:00 PM

Case resolution meeting: present investigation findings to VP HR and legal counsel for a substantiated policy violation; recommend disciplinary action; draft outcome communication.

Grievance & Complaint Management, Disciplinary Action & Performance Improvement

2:30 PM

Policy update project: draft revisions to social media policy based on recent NLRB guidance; circulate for stakeholder review; prepare training FAQ for managers.

Policy Development & Management, Compliance & Mandatory Training

3:30 PM

Leave administration: review three FMLA certification requests, assess eligibility, coordinate with payroll on leave-related pay adjustments, update leave tracking system.

Workplace Accommodations & Leave Management, Payroll Processing

4:30 PM

Monthly ER metrics preparation: case volume trends, average resolution time, open case aging, category breakdown; flag patterns (3 complaints against same manager) for HRBP attention.

Grievance & Complaint Management, Workforce Analytics & Reporting

P08: DEI Program Manager

The DEI Program Manager balances strategic initiatives with operational programming, ensuring diversity, equity, and inclusion are embedded into business processes and organizational culture.

Time

Activity

Processes Engaged

8:00 AM

Review weekly DEI dashboard: representation metrics by level and function, hiring funnel diversity data, promotion equity analysis, inclusion index trends from latest pulse.

DEI Strategy & Metrics, Workforce Analytics & Reporting

9:00 AM

ERG leaders monthly sync: review Q1 programming calendars for five ERGs, discuss budget allocation, plan cross-ERG collaboration event for Heritage Month, gather membership feedback.

ERG (Employee Resource Group) Management

10:00 AM

Inclusive hiring audit: work with VP TA to analyze interview panel diversity, review job description language screening results, assess slate diversity compliance by hiring manager.

DEI Strategy & Metrics, Interview & Selection

11:00 AM

Pay equity review with Comp & Ben Manager: review regression analysis results by gender and ethnicity; identify remediation recommendations; prepare board-ready summary.

DEI Strategy & Metrics, Compensation Benchmarking & Band Design

12:30 PM

Inclusive leadership training facilitation: deliver 90-minute workshop for people manager cohort on mitigating bias in performance reviews and creating psychological safety.

Leadership Development Programs, Annual/Semi-Annual Performance Review

2:30 PM

External reporting preparation: compile EEO-1 data, draft corporate social responsibility report DEI section, prepare Disability Equality Index submission materials.

DEI Strategy & Metrics, Workforce Analytics & Reporting

3:30 PM

Strategic planning session with CHRO: present proposed DEI goals for next fiscal year, discuss supplier diversity integration, review benchmarking against peer organizations.

DEI Strategy & Metrics, Strategic Workforce Planning

4:30 PM

Community engagement: finalize partnership agreements with two HBCUs for internship pipeline; review scholarship program applications; coordinate mentorship program matching.

Sourcing & Candidate Pipeline, ERG (Employee Resource Group) Management

P09: People Analytics Analyst

The People Analytics Analyst transforms raw HR data into actionable intelligence, building models, dashboards, and ad-hoc analyses that drive evidence-based decision-making across the HR function.

Time

Activity

Processes Engaged

8:00 AM

Data pipeline monitoring: check ETL job status for overnight HRIS-to-warehouse sync; investigate data quality alert on missing termination records; fix schema mapping error.

Workforce Analytics & Reporting, Employee Data Management (HRIS)

9:00 AM

Attrition model refresh: retrain predictive flight-risk model with Q4 data; evaluate feature importance changes; update risk scores for all active employees; validate against recent departures.

Workforce Analytics & Reporting

10:00 AM

HRBP request: ad-hoc analysis on engineering team productivity metrics correlated with engagement scores; build visualization showing relationship between manager check-in frequency and retention.

Workforce Analytics & Reporting, Employee Engagement Surveys

11:00 AM

Engagement survey deep-dive: run driver analysis on latest survey results; identify top 5 factors differentiating high-engagement vs. low-engagement teams; segment by tenure and level.

Employee Engagement Surveys, Workforce Analytics & Reporting

12:30 PM

Pay equity support: build multi-variate regression model for Comp & Ben Manager; control for tenure, performance, location, and job family; generate statistical significance reports.

Workforce Analytics & Reporting, DEI Strategy & Metrics

2:00 PM

Executive dashboard development: build new Looker dashboard for CHRO showing real-time headcount, attrition, hiring velocity, cost-per-hire, and diversity metrics with drill-down by BU.

Workforce Analytics & Reporting, Strategic Workforce Planning

3:30 PM

Workforce planning support: run scenario models for CHRO showing headcount projections under three growth scenarios; model impact of 5% vs. 10% attrition on hiring demand.

Strategic Workforce Planning, Workforce Analytics & Reporting

4:30 PM

Data governance review: audit user access permissions for HR data warehouse; update data dictionary with new fields from LMS integration; review data retention policies with legal.

Workforce Analytics & Reporting, HR Technology Governance

P10: Payroll & Compliance Specialist

The Payroll & Compliance Specialist manages the highest-stakes operational process in HR – getting people paid accurately and on time – while navigating a complex web of tax jurisdictions, labor laws, and regulatory requirements.

Time

Activity

Processes Engaged

7:00 AM

Pre-payroll validation: review time & attendance exception reports; verify overtime calculations for non-exempt employees; check for missing timesheets and send manager reminders.

Payroll Processing

8:00 AM

Process employee changes for payroll: new hire records from HRIS, salary adjustments, benefit deduction changes, garnishment orders, termination final pay calculations.

Payroll Processing, Employee Data Management (HRIS)

9:30 AM

Payroll run execution: submit gross-to-net processing batch; monitor for calculation errors; validate tax withholding for multi-state employees; review imputed income calculations.

Payroll Processing

10:30 AM

Post-payroll reconciliation: compare payroll register to GL journal entries; reconcile tax liability accounts; verify benefit deduction totals with carrier invoices.

Payroll Processing, Benefits Administration

12:00 PM

Compliance review: audit I-9 documentation for recent hires; review state-specific labor law updates (new minimum wage in three states, new paid family leave requirement in one); update compliance tracker.

Policy Development & Management, Compliance & Mandatory Training

1:30 PM

Garnishment processing: review three new wage garnishment orders (child support, tax levy, student loan); calculate disposable income; set up deductions; send required notifications.

Payroll Processing, Workplace Accommodations & Leave Management

3:00 PM

Year-end planning: begin W-2 reconciliation preparation; review equity compensation tax treatment for vested RSUs; coordinate with finance on stock option exercise reporting.

Payroll Processing, Equity & Long-Term Incentive Management

4:30 PM

Employee query resolution: respond to five paycheck discrepancy inquiries; explain tax withholding changes from W-4 updates; assist with HSA contribution corrections.

HR Service Delivery (Tier 0/1/2), Payroll Processing

P11: Recruiter / Talent Acquisition Partner

The Recruiter operates at the execution level of talent acquisition, managing a portfolio of open requisitions through every stage of the hiring funnel while building relationships with candidates and hiring managers.

Time

Activity

Processes Engaged

8:00 AM

ATS inbox review: screen 25 new applications for three priority roles; advance qualified candidates to phone screen stage; send regret communications to non-fits.

Sourcing & Candidate Pipeline, Interview & Selection

9:00 AM

Sourcing block: conduct Boolean searches on LinkedIn Recruiter for a hard-to-fill ML engineer role; craft personalized outreach messages; review referral submissions from employee referral portal.

Sourcing & Candidate Pipeline

10:00 AM

Phone screens: conduct three 30-minute phone screens; assess technical qualifications, cultural alignment, and compensation expectations; update scorecards in ATS.

Interview & Selection

11:30 AM

Hiring manager sync: debrief on this week’s onsite interviews; calibrate scorecard feedback; discuss pipeline health for two roles that have been open 45+ days.

Interview & Selection, Requisition & Approval

12:30 PM

Interview scheduling: coordinate panel interviews for four candidates across two time zones; book conference rooms; send preparation materials to interviewers.

Interview & Selection

1:30 PM

Candidate nurturing: follow up with three passive candidates in pipeline; share company blog post and upcoming meetup invite; update CRM notes.

Sourcing & Candidate Pipeline

2:30 PM

Offer preparation: work with Compensation to build offer package for selected Product Manager candidate; model base, bonus, equity, and sign-on; draft offer letter for approval.

Offer & Pre-boarding, Compensation Benchmarking & Band Design

3:30 PM

Offer negotiation: verbal offer call with candidate; address counter-offer on base salary; escalate equity adjustment request to VP TA; confirm expected start date.

Offer & Pre-boarding

4:30 PM

Pre-boarding coordination: trigger background check for accepted candidate; send pre-boarding checklist to HR Ops; coordinate with IT for equipment setup; assign onboarding buddy.

Offer & Pre-boarding, Onboarding & Integration

P12: HRIS / HR Technology Analyst

The HRIS / HR Technology Analyst ensures the technology backbone of HR operates reliably, managing system configurations, integrations, data flows, and the evaluation of new tools that enable the function.

Time

Activity

Processes Engaged

8:00 AM

System monitoring: check HRIS uptime dashboard, review integration error logs (payroll sync, benefits feed, ATS handoff), resolve two failed API calls from overnight batch.

Employee Data Management (HRIS), HR Technology Governance

9:00 AM

Support ticket triage: address five open HRIS support tickets (user access request, report not generating, self-service password reset issue, incorrect manager hierarchy, missing cost center).

Employee Data Management (HRIS), HR Service Delivery (Tier 0/1/2)

10:00 AM

Configuration change: build new approval workflow in Workday for off-cycle compensation changes per Comp & Ben Manager’s requirements; test in sandbox; document configuration.

Employee Data Management (HRIS), Annual Compensation Review (Merit & Promotion)

11:00 AM

Open enrollment system readiness: configure benefits enrollment windows, load new plan options, test life-event triggers, validate dependent eligibility rules with Comp & Ben.

Benefits Administration, Employee Data Management (HRIS)

12:30 PM

Data migration project: prepare data mapping for LMS integration; write transformation rules for employee ID, manager hierarchy, and job code alignment; schedule UAT.

HR Technology Governance, Employee Data Management (HRIS)

2:00 PM

Vendor evaluation: demo new employee engagement survey platform; assess API capabilities, SSO integration, data export formats, GDPR compliance, and pricing model.

HR Technology Governance, Employee Engagement Surveys

3:00 PM

Report development: build custom report for People Analytics team joining HRIS headcount data with ATS pipeline data; optimize SQL query performance for monthly refresh.

Workforce Analytics & Reporting, Employee Data Management (HRIS)

4:00 PM

Security audit preparation: review user role assignments in HRIS, flag excessive-privilege accounts, generate access log report for SOX compliance, update documentation for auditors.

Employee Data Management (HRIS), HR Technology Governance

5:00 PM

Release management: review upcoming HRIS vendor release notes; assess impact on custom configurations; plan regression testing; communicate changes to HR Ops team.

HR Technology Governance

1.4 Detailed Process Catalog

Each process domain is expanded below with its constituent processes. For every process, the following dimensions are documented: the primary persona(s) responsible, the end-to-end sequence of activities, the unstructured knowledge and information datasets that support the process, and the structured data products sourced from data lakes or data warehouses. This catalog is designed to feed directly into Chapter 2, where each process is mapped to agentic automation opportunities.

Process Domain 1 - Workforce Planning & Organizational Design

Domain Owners: CHRO, HRBP

Process 1.1: Strategic Workforce Planning

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

CHRO, HRBP

Activity Sequence

Analyze business strategy → Assess current workforce capacity & capability → Forecast future talent demand → Identify gaps (skills, headcount, location) → Develop supply-side strategies (build, buy, borrow, bot) → Create workforce plan → Align with finance on budget → Gain executive approval → Cascade to BU-level execution plans

Unstructured Knowledge

Industry labor market reports, competitor org charts, strategic plan documents, board presentations, external consulting studies

Structured Data Products

Headcount actuals & forecasts, skills inventory, attrition trends, cost-per-hire, labor cost models, scenario planning outputs

Process 1.2: Organization Design & Restructuring

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

CHRO, HRBP

Activity Sequence

Receive business trigger (M&A, growth, efficiency) → Analyze current org structure → Benchmark spans & layers → Design future-state org → Role mapping & job architecture alignment → Impact assessment (RIF, redeployment, reskilling) → Change management plan → Communication cascade → Execute transitions → Monitor stabilization metrics

Unstructured Knowledge

Org design frameworks (Galbraith Star Model), change management playbooks, communication templates, transition guides

Structured Data Products

Org hierarchy data, span-of-control metrics, role catalog, FTE counts by level/function/location, cost modeling

Process 1.3: Job Architecture & Role Management

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

HRBP, Comp & Ben Mgr

Activity Sequence

Define job families & functions → Create job levels & career tracks → Write standardized job descriptions → Map competency profiles per level → Align with compensation bands → Validate with business leaders → Publish in HRIS → Periodic review & refresh

Unstructured Knowledge

Job description library, competency frameworks, career path documentation, external benchmarking reports (Mercer, Radford)

Structured Data Products

Job code master, grade/level mapping, compensation band linkage, filled/open position counts per job family

Process Domain 2 - Talent Acquisition & Onboarding

Domain Owners: VP TA, Recruiter

Process 2.1: Requisition & Approval

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

Recruiter, HRBP

Activity Sequence

Hiring manager submits requisition → HRBP validates against workforce plan → Finance confirms budget → Compensation reviews grade/salary range → Approvals workflow (manager chain + HR) → Requisition activated in ATS → Assign recruiter

Unstructured Knowledge

Requisition justification memos, approval email threads, headcount authorization policies

Structured Data Products

Requisition status data, approval timestamps, budget allocation, position ID, cost center mapping

Process 2.2: Sourcing & Candidate Pipeline

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

Recruiter, VP TA

Activity Sequence

Develop sourcing strategy (channels, events, referrals) → Post job internally/externally → Proactive outreach (LinkedIn, databases) → Referral program activation → Candidate screening (resume/AI) → Build talent pipeline → Nurture passive candidates

Unstructured Knowledge

Sourcing playbooks, employer brand content, candidate outreach templates, job board analytics, referral program guidelines

Structured Data Products

Source-of-hire data, pipeline stage counts, channel conversion rates, cost-per-source, diversity pipeline metrics

Process 2.3: Interview & Selection

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

Recruiter, Hiring Manager

Activity Sequence

Phone/video screen → Technical/skills assessment → Panel interviews (structured) → Hiring manager interview → Collect scorecards → Debrief & calibration → Reference & background checks → Selection decision → Document rationale

Unstructured Knowledge

Interview guides & question banks, scorecard rubrics, assessment frameworks, reference check templates, debrief notes

Structured Data Products

Interview scores, pass-through rates by stage, interviewer calibration data, time-in-stage, candidate feedback surveys

Process 2.4: Offer & Pre-boarding

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

Recruiter, Comp & Ben Mgr

Activity Sequence

Determine compensation package → Generate offer letter → Approvals → Extend verbal offer → Negotiate (if needed) → Send written offer → Candidate accepts → Initiate background verification → Pre-boarding checklist (IT, facilities, buddy assignment) → Welcome communication

Unstructured Knowledge

Offer letter templates, negotiation guidelines, relocation policies, pre-boarding checklists, welcome packets

Structured Data Products

Offer acceptance rates, offer-to-join ratio, compensation benchmarking data, start-date tracking, background check status

Process 2.5: Onboarding & Integration

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

HR Ops Mgr, L&D Mgr

Activity Sequence

Day-1 logistics (badge, equipment, access) → Orientation program → HR paperwork & compliance training → Buddy/mentor pairing → Role-specific training plan → 30/60/90-day milestones → Manager check-ins → Onboarding survey → Probation review → Full integration confirmation

Unstructured Knowledge

Onboarding playbooks, orientation decks, culture handbooks, department-specific training materials, FAQ documents

Structured Data Products

Onboarding completion rates, time-to-productivity, new-hire satisfaction scores, probation pass/fail, early attrition rates

Process Domain 3 - Performance & Talent Management

Domain Owners: HRBP, CHRO

Process 3.1: Goal Setting & OKR Alignment

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

HRBP, People Managers

Activity Sequence

Cascade company objectives → Manager-employee goal discussion → Draft individual goals/OKRs → Align with team & department goals → Manager approval → Publish in performance system → Quarterly check-ins & recalibration → Link to development plans

Unstructured Knowledge

OKR methodology guides, goal-setting workshops, alignment templates, best-practice examples

Structured Data Products

Goal completion rates, OKR scores, alignment coverage (% employees with goals), goal-revision frequency

Process 3.2: Continuous Feedback & Check-ins

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

HRBP, People Managers

Activity Sequence

Schedule regular 1:1s → Prepare talking points → Discuss progress, blockers, development → Document key takeaways → Peer feedback requests → Real-time recognition → Aggregate feedback trends → Input to performance review

Unstructured Knowledge

1:1 meeting templates, feedback frameworks (SBI, COIN), coaching guides, recognition program guidelines

Structured Data Products

Check-in completion rates, feedback frequency, recognition counts, sentiment scores from feedback text

Process 3.3: Annual/Semi-Annual Performance Review

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

HRBP, CHRO

Activity Sequence

Launch review cycle → Self-assessment → Manager assessment → 360-degree feedback (optional) → Calibration sessions → Final rating assignment → Performance conversation → Link to compensation & development → Document & sign-off → Appeal process

Unstructured Knowledge

Review form templates, calibration facilitation guides, rating scale definitions, conversation guides, appeal procedures

Structured Data Products

Performance ratings distribution, calibration adjustments, rating-by-demographics, forced ranking outputs, review completion rates

Process 3.4: Succession Planning

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

CHRO, HRBP

Activity Sequence

Identify critical roles → Assess bench strength → Evaluate potential (9-box) → Identify successors (ready-now, 1-2 years, 2-5 years) → Create development plans for successors → Executive review & validation → Monitor progress → Update annually

Unstructured Knowledge

9-box assessment criteria, successor profile templates, executive talent review presentations, development action plans

Structured Data Products

Successor pipeline coverage ratio, bench strength by role, readiness levels, development plan completion, successor turnover

Process 3.5: High-Potential (HiPo) Program Management

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

CHRO, L&D Mgr

Activity Sequence

Define HiPo criteria → Nomination & assessment → Selection committee review → Communicate selection → Design accelerated development (stretch assignments, mentoring, executive exposure) → Track progress → Graduate to successor pools → Program evaluation

Unstructured Knowledge

HiPo assessment rubrics, development journey maps, mentoring program guidelines, executive sponsor briefings

Structured Data Products

HiPo pool demographics, promotion rates of HiPos vs. non-HiPos, retention rates, program ROI, development milestone completion

Process Domain 4 - Compensation, Benefits & Total Rewards

Domain Owners: Comp & Ben Mgr, CHRO

Process 4.1: Compensation Benchmarking & Band Design

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

Comp & Ben Mgr

Activity Sequence

Select survey sources → Participate in compensation surveys → Analyze market data by role/geo → Define compensation philosophy (percentile targeting) → Design salary bands → Map jobs to bands → Compa-ratio analysis → Executive approval → Communicate to managers

Unstructured Knowledge

Compensation survey reports (Mercer, Radford, Payscale), philosophy statements, communication toolkits

Structured Data Products

Market data cuts, salary band tables, compa-ratios, pay-mix data (base/variable/equity), geographic differentials

Process 4.2: Annual Compensation Review (Merit & Promotion)

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

Comp & Ben Mgr, HRBP

Activity Sequence

Budget allocation from finance → Manager recommendations → HR review for equity & consistency → Calibration sessions → Pay equity analysis → Final approvals → Generate compensation letters → Manager delivery conversations → Process in payroll

Unstructured Knowledge

Merit increase guidelines, promotion criteria documents, manager talking points, equity analysis reports

Structured Data Products

Merit budgets, increase percentages by performance tier, promotion rates, pay equity gap metrics, total compensation changes

Process 4.3: Benefits Administration

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

Comp & Ben Mgr, HR Ops Mgr

Activity Sequence

Design benefits portfolio → Vendor selection & negotiation → Open enrollment campaign → Employee enrollment → Life event changes → Claims support → Utilization monitoring → Annual renewal → Compliance reporting (ACA, ERISA, etc.)

Unstructured Knowledge

Benefits guides, plan comparison documents, vendor contracts, enrollment FAQs, compliance checklists

Structured Data Products

Enrollment rates by plan, utilization data, cost trends, dependent coverage, claims data, employee satisfaction scores

Process 4.4: Equity & Long-Term Incentive Management

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

Comp & Ben Mgr, CHRO

Activity Sequence

Design equity plan → Board/committee approval → Grant allocation → Participant communication → Vesting schedule management → Exercise/settlement processing → Tax withholding → Reporting (SEC, accounting) → Termination handling

Unstructured Knowledge

Equity plan documents, grant letters, tax treatment guides, SEC filing templates, employee education materials

Structured Data Products

Grant ledger, vesting schedules, exercise data, dilution metrics, Black-Scholes valuations, share pool balances

Process Domain 5 - Learning & Development

Domain Owners: L&D Mgr

Process 5.1: Training Needs Analysis

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

L&D Mgr, HRBP

Activity Sequence

Gather inputs (performance data, business priorities, skills gaps) → Survey employees & managers → Analyze competency gaps → Prioritize learning needs → Define learning objectives → Align with budget → Create annual L&D plan → Stakeholder sign-off

Unstructured Knowledge

Skills frameworks, competency assessments, employee survey free-text responses, industry skills trend reports

Structured Data Products

Skills-gap heat maps, training request logs, competency assessment scores, budget allocation by domain

Process 5.2: Learning Content Development & Curation

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

L&D Mgr

Activity Sequence

Define content strategy → Develop in-house content (ILT, e-learning, microlearning) → Curate external content (LinkedIn Learning, Coursera, etc.) → Quality review → Publish in LMS/LXP → Assign to learning paths → Maintain & retire outdated content

Unstructured Knowledge

Instructional design frameworks (ADDIE, SAM), storyboards, facilitator guides, vendor content catalogs

Structured Data Products

Content inventory, completion rates by course, learner ratings, content utilization, cost per learner hour

Process 5.3: Compliance & Mandatory Training

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

L&D Mgr, Payroll Specialist

Activity Sequence

Identify regulatory requirements by jurisdiction → Map to training modules → Assign to applicable populations → Set deadlines → Track completion → Send reminders & escalations → Generate compliance certificates → Audit reporting

Unstructured Knowledge

Regulatory requirement matrices, training policy documents, audit checklists, certification templates

Structured Data Products

Compliance completion rates, overdue counts, training assignment rules, audit trail logs, certification expiry dates

Process 5.4: Leadership Development Programs

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

L&D Mgr, CHRO

Activity Sequence

Identify target populations → Design program curriculum → Select facilitators/coaches → Participant nomination & selection → Program delivery (workshops, coaching, action learning) → Capstone projects → Evaluate outcomes (Kirkpatrick model) → Alumni network

Unstructured Knowledge

Program brochures, facilitator guides, coaching frameworks, capstone project briefs, alumni engagement plans

Structured Data Products

Participant demographics, program costs, pre/post assessments, promotion rates of graduates, engagement lift

Process Domain 6 - Employee Relations & Compliance

Domain Owners: ER Specialist, Payroll Specialist

Process 6.1: Grievance & Complaint Management

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

ER Specialist

Activity Sequence

Employee raises complaint (hotline, HR, manager) → Intake & categorize → Assign case owner → Preliminary assessment → Investigation (interviews, evidence) → Findings & recommendations → Resolution actions → Communicate to parties → Monitor for retaliation → Close case & document

Unstructured Knowledge

Investigation protocols, interview question guides, evidence collection checklists, legal hold procedures, case report templates

Structured Data Products

Case volume & type, resolution time, outcome categories, repeat offender flags, hotline utilization, anonymous vs. named ratio

Process 6.2: Disciplinary Action & Performance Improvement

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

ER Specialist, HRBP

Activity Sequence

Identify performance/conduct issue → Informal coaching → Verbal warning (documented) → Written warning → Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) → Monitor PIP progress → PIP outcome decision → Termination (if unresolved) → Documentation & legal review

Unstructured Knowledge

Progressive discipline policy, PIP templates, coaching conversation guides, termination checklists, legal review protocols

Structured Data Products

PIP initiation & success rates, disciplinary action counts by type, time-to-resolution, termination reasons, legal challenge rates

Process 6.3: Policy Development & Management

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

ER Specialist, CHRO

Activity Sequence

Identify policy need → Draft policy → Legal review → Stakeholder consultation → Executive approval → Communication & training → Publish in policy repository → Periodic review cycle → Update & version control

Unstructured Knowledge

Policy templates, regulatory guidance documents, industry benchmarking, policy acknowledgment forms

Structured Data Products

Policy inventory, review schedule compliance, acknowledgment rates, policy exception logs, change history

Process 6.4: Workplace Accommodations & Leave Management

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

ER Specialist, HR Ops Mgr

Activity Sequence

Employee requests accommodation/leave → Gather documentation → Assess eligibility (ADA, FMLA, local laws) → Interactive process → Determine reasonable accommodation → Implement & communicate → Track leave balances → Return-to-work process → Ongoing monitoring

Unstructured Knowledge

ADA interactive process guides, FMLA eligibility flowcharts, accommodation request forms, medical certification forms

Structured Data Products

Leave utilization by type, accommodation request volumes, approval/denial rates, return-to-work timelines, cost of accommodations

Process Domain 7 - Employee Engagement & Culture

Domain Owners: HRBP, CHRO, DEI Mgr

Process 7.1: Employee Engagement Surveys

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

HRBP, People Analytics

Activity Sequence

Design survey instrument → Communicate purpose & confidentiality → Launch survey → Monitor response rates → Close survey → Analyze results (overall, by cut) → Share results with leaders → Cascade to teams → Action planning workshops → Track action plan execution → Pulse surveys for follow-up

Unstructured Knowledge

Survey question banks, communication templates, action planning facilitation guides, best-practice sharing forums

Structured Data Products

Engagement scores (eNPS, dimensions), response rates, year-over-year trends, demographic cuts, action plan completion rates, correlation analysis

Process 7.2: Recognition & Rewards Programs

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

HRBP, HR Ops Mgr

Activity Sequence

Define recognition philosophy → Design program tiers (peer-to-peer, manager, spot awards, milestone) → Select platform → Launch & communicate → Monitor adoption → Budget management → Program effectiveness review → Iterate

Unstructured Knowledge

Program design documents, nomination forms, award descriptions, success stories, vendor proposals

Structured Data Products

Recognition frequency, award distribution by team/level, budget utilization, correlation with engagement & retention

Process 7.3: Internal Communications & Culture Initiatives

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

CHRO, HRBP, DEI Mgr

Activity Sequence

Develop communication strategy → Content calendar creation → Town halls & all-hands planning → Intranet/newsletter content → Culture initiative design (values workshops, team-building) → Execute → Measure reach & sentiment → Feedback loop

Unstructured Knowledge

Communication plans, town hall scripts, newsletter archives, culture playbooks, event planning guides

Structured Data Products

Communication reach metrics, intranet analytics, town hall attendance, sentiment analysis, culture survey results

Process Domain 8 - HR Operations & Shared Services

Domain Owners: HR Ops Mgr, Payroll Specialist, HRIS Analyst

Process 8.1: Employee Data Management (HRIS)

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

HR Ops Mgr, HRIS Analyst

Activity Sequence

New hire data entry → Ongoing data maintenance (promotions, transfers, personal changes) → Data validation & audits → Role-based access management → Integration management (payroll, benefits, finance) → Reporting & analytics extracts → Data retention & archival

Unstructured Knowledge

Data governance policies, data dictionary, HRIS user guides, integration specifications, audit checklists

Structured Data Products

Employee master data, position management, organizational hierarchy, data quality scores, integration logs, audit trails

Process 8.2: HR Service Delivery (Tier 0/1/2)

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

HR Ops Mgr

Activity Sequence

Employee submits query (self-service portal, chatbot, email, phone) → Tier-0 self-service resolution → Tier-1 generalist handling → Tier-2 specialist escalation → Case tracking & SLA monitoring → Knowledge base maintenance → Continuous improvement

Unstructured Knowledge

Knowledge base articles, FAQ documents, process SOPs, case resolution scripts, escalation matrices

Structured Data Products

Case volumes by category, resolution times, first-contact resolution rate, SLA adherence, CSAT scores, chatbot deflection rates

Process 8.3: Payroll Processing

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

Payroll Specialist

Activity Sequence

Collect time & attendance data → Validate inputs (new hires, changes, terminations) → Process gross-to-net calculations → Apply deductions (benefits, taxes, garnishments) → Run payroll → Quality checks → Approval → Bank file generation → Payslip distribution → GL posting → Tax filings & remittances → Year-end processing (W-2, etc.)

Unstructured Knowledge

Payroll processing calendars, tax code reference guides, garnishment handling procedures, year-end checklists

Structured Data Products

Payroll registers, tax withholding data, deduction details, GL journal entries, payroll error rates, processing timelines

Process 8.4: Offboarding & Exit Management

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

HR Ops Mgr, HRBP

Activity Sequence

Resignation/termination notification → Exit interview scheduling → Knowledge transfer plan → Asset recovery (laptop, badge, keys) → System access revocation → Final pay calculation (PTO payout, severance) → Benefits continuation (COBRA) → Exit interview → Alumni network enrollment → Compliance documentation

Unstructured Knowledge

Exit interview questionnaires, offboarding checklists, severance agreement templates, COBRA notices, knowledge transfer templates

Structured Data Products

Exit reason codes, exit survey data, regrettable vs. non-regrettable attrition, time-to-backfill, final settlement processing times

Process Domain 9 - DEI & Belonging

Domain Owners: DEI Mgr, CHRO

Process 9.1: DEI Strategy & Metrics

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

DEI Mgr, CHRO

Activity Sequence

Assess current state (representation, inclusion index) → Set DEI goals → Develop strategy & initiatives → Secure executive sponsorship → Allocate budget → Execute programs → Track metrics → Report to leadership & externally → Iterate

Unstructured Knowledge

DEI strategy documents, industry benchmarks, ERG charters, inclusive leadership frameworks, external reporting templates

Structured Data Products

Representation data by level/function/demographics, inclusion index scores, pay-gap metrics, hiring funnel diversity, promotion equity

Process 9.2: ERG (Employee Resource Group) Management

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

DEI Mgr

Activity Sequence

Establish ERG governance → Chapter formation → Leadership selection → Annual planning & budgeting → Event programming → Membership engagement → Business impact measurement → Executive sponsor alignment → Annual review

Unstructured Knowledge

ERG playbooks, event planning guides, budget request templates, impact reports, member feedback surveys

Structured Data Products

ERG membership counts, event attendance, budget utilization, member engagement scores, business impact metrics

Process Domain 10 - People Analytics & HR Technology

Domain Owners: People Analytics, HRIS Analyst

Process 10.1: Workforce Analytics & Reporting

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

People Analytics

Activity Sequence

Define metrics taxonomy → Build data pipelines (HRIS, ATS, LMS, engagement) → Create dashboards & scorecards → Scheduled reporting (monthly, quarterly) → Ad-hoc analysis requests → Predictive modeling (attrition, performance) → Insights presentation → Action recommendations

Unstructured Knowledge

Metrics definitions, data dictionaries, analysis request templates, visualization best practices, data ethics guidelines

Structured Data Products

Integrated workforce data warehouse, KPI dashboards, predictive model outputs, benchmarking databases, survey data lakes

Process 10.2: HR Technology Governance

Dimension

Details

Primary Persona(s)

HRIS Analyst

Activity Sequence

Maintain HR tech landscape inventory → Evaluate new tool requests → Vendor assessment & selection → Implementation project management → Change management & training → Integration management → License optimization → Contract renewals → Decommissioning

Unstructured Knowledge

RFP templates, vendor evaluation scorecards, implementation playbooks, change management plans, training materials

Structured Data Products

Tech stack inventory, license utilization, integration maps, vendor spend, user adoption metrics, support ticket volumes

Chapter 2 — HR / PeopleOps Systems Landscape & Process Transformation Roadmap

2.1 Consolidated HR Technology Landscape

The following table catalogs the complete HR technology ecosystem that Gemini Enterprise agents interact with across all ten process domains. For each system category, it shows typical products in the market, the current role the system plays in HR operations, and how that role changes in the transformed state. This is a single, consolidated view — it replaces per-domain systems tables and provides the foundation for the disposition analysis and phased roadmap that follow.

System Category

Typical Products

As-Is Role

To-Be Role

HRIS / HCM

Workday, SAP SuccessFactors, Oracle HCM, BambooHR, UKG, Ceridian Dayforce

Core employee master data, org hierarchy, position management, employee lifecycle transactions; primary system of record for most HR data

Remains the system of record; agents connect via API for real-time data reads and writes; humans continue to use HRIS for complex configuration but routine transactions are agent-orchestrated

Applicant Tracking System (ATS)

Greenhouse, Lever, iCIMS, SmartRecruiters, Workday Recruiting, Taleo

Requisition management, candidate pipeline tracking, interview scheduling coordination, offer management; recruiters spend majority of their day in the ATS

Remains the recruiting system of record; agents read/write pipeline data, trigger workflows, and manage candidate communications via API; recruiters interact through both ATS and Gemini

Payroll System

ADP, Ceridian, Paychex, UKG, SAP Payroll, Workday Payroll, InfoTech

Gross-to-net processing, tax calculations, deduction management, bank file generation, tax filings; receives inputs from HRIS and T&A manually or via batch feeds

Remains the payroll processing engine; agents validate inputs pre-run and reconcile outputs post-run via API; processing logic unchanged

Time & Attendance

Kronos/UKG, ADP Time, Workday Time, Deputy, When I Work

Time tracking, shift scheduling, overtime calculation, absence recording; feeds payroll with time data

Remains the time capture system; agents read exception data for payroll validation and leave tracking

Performance Management

Lattice, 15Five, Culture Amp, Workday Performance, SuccessFactors Performance & Goals, Betterworks

Goal tracking, review cycle management, feedback collection, calibration support; adoption and data quality vary widely across organizations

Remains the performance system of record; agents read/write goals, generate review narratives, track cycle completion, and feed calibration analytics via API

Learning Management (LMS/LXP)

Cornerstone, Degreed, Docebo, Absorb, Workday Learning, MindTickle, Udemy Business

Training catalog, course assignment, completion tracking, compliance certification; content delivery platform for both mandatory and elective learning

Remains the learning delivery and tracking platform; agents auto-assign training, track compliance, recommend personalized paths, and generate content summaries via API

Compensation & Market Data

Payfactors, Payscale, Mercer WIN, Radford, Salary.com, CompAnalyst

Market benchmarking data, salary survey participation; data downloaded and analyzed manually in spreadsheets by compensation analysts

Agents access market data via API or structured feeds for real-time benchmarking, offer modeling, and pay equity analysis; eliminates manual data download and spreadsheet modeling

Benefits Platforms & Carriers

bswift, Benefitfocus, PlanSource, carrier portals (Aetna, Cigna, UHC, Kaiser)

Plan enrollment, claims processing, eligibility management; employees interact during open enrollment and life events; HR manages carrier relationships and data feeds

Agents connect for real-time plan information, enrollment guidance, life-event processing, and utilization analytics; carrier platforms remain for claims processing

Equity Administration

Shareworks (Morgan Stanley), Carta, E*Trade, Fidelity Stock Plan Services

Grant management, vesting schedules, exercise processing, tax withholding, SEC reporting; specialized system used by compensation team and finance

Agents access for equity modeling, participant communication generation, and cross-system total rewards optimization; administration platform remains for processing

Sourcing Platforms

LinkedIn Recruiter, SeekOut, HireEZ, Entelo, Hiretual

Candidate identification and outreach; recruiters manually search, message, and track responses; each platform used independently

Agents orchestrate search and outreach across platforms via API; personalized messaging at scale; response tracking unified

Background Check Vendors

Checkr, Sterling, HireRight, GoodHire, First Advantage

Identity verification, criminal background, employment verification, education verification; recruiter manually initiates in vendor portal after offer acceptance

Agents auto-initiate checks via API upon offer acceptance; status monitored automatically with exception alerts

Case Management (ER)

HR Acuity, EthicsPoint (NAVEX), i-Sight, ServiceNow HR

Employee relations case intake, investigation tracking, resolution documentation; used by ER specialists for grievance and complaint management

Agents auto-classify cases, detect patterns, recommend investigation approaches, and generate analytics; case system remains for documentation and audit trail

Survey & Engagement

Qualtrics, Culture Amp, Peakon (Workday), Glint, SurveyMonkey

Engagement survey deployment, pulse surveys, results analysis; People Analytics team analyzes results and prepares reports for leadership

Agents assist survey design, generate launch communications, perform multi-dimensional analysis, identify drivers, and correlate with business outcomes via API

Recognition Platforms

Bonusly, Achievers, Workhuman, Kudos, Nectar

Peer-to-peer and manager recognition, milestone celebrations, rewards redemption; adoption varies by team and manager

Agents send proactive nudges to low-recognition managers, auto-generate milestone celebrations, and analyze recognition equity and correlation with retention

IT Ticketing & Service Desk

ServiceNow, Jira Service Management, Freshservice, Zendesk

IT equipment provisioning, access management, HR service desk case routing; HR manually creates tickets for onboarding/offboarding tasks

Agents auto-create provisioning and deprovisioning tickets via API with role-based specs; case routing automated with context

Financial Planning

Anaplan, Adaptive Insights (Workday), Oracle Planning Cloud, Planful

Headcount budgets, workforce cost modeling, scenario planning; finance team manages; HR provides inputs via spreadsheets or email

Agents access budget and forecast data via API for real-time workforce plan validation and cost modeling; eliminates the HR-finance email loop for budget confirmation

Calendar & Email

Google Workspace (Gmail, Calendar), Microsoft 365 (Outlook, Teams)

The primary coordination mechanism for HR — interview scheduling, approval chains, candidate communications, employee queries, manager coaching; HR professionals spend significant time in email

Agents read calendars and send communications via API; email volume for coordination dramatically reduced; humans use email for relationship-based communication only

Knowledge Base / Intranet

SharePoint, Confluence, Notion, Google Sites, Guru

Policy documents, SOPs, FAQ content, onboarding materials; searched manually by HR representatives when answering employee questions; content freshness varies

Agents use as searchable corpus for policy Q&A and employee self-service; content becomes the knowledge foundation for conversational AI; freshness becomes critical

Spreadsheets & Manual Workarounds

Excel, Google Sheets, email threads, shared drives, Word/PowerPoint templates

The connective tissue of HR operations: headcount trackers, scorecard aggregation, onboarding checklists, compensation modeling, calibration worksheets, reporting dashboards, scenario models, approval tracking, candidate CRMs, leave trackers

Largely eliminated — agents replace humans as the integration layer between systems of record; data flows through APIs instead of copy-paste; tracking happens in source systems instead of shadow spreadsheets

Predictive Analytics & ML

None (or basic BI dashboards)

Typically does not exist — predictive attrition, skills-based redeployment matching, hiring outcome correlation, engagement driver analysis, workforce scenario modeling are either not done or done manually as one-off consulting projects

New capabilities introduced via custom agents on ADK runtime with Vertex AI: flight-risk prediction, skills intelligence, real-time pay equity analysis, engagement-to-outcome correlation, workforce scenario modeling

Agentic Orchestration Layer

None

Does not exist — humans serve as the integration layer, manually coordinating across 10-15 systems via email, spreadsheets, and context-switching

Gemini Enterprise becomes the unified interaction layer; agents orchestrate workflows across systems of record; humans shift from data movers to decision makers and relationship builders

2.2 What Really Transforms

A critical question in any transformation is: what happens to our existing technology investments? The answer for Gemini Enterprise agentic transformation is clear and distinct from traditional system replacement projects. The table below summarizes the three disposition categories and their implications.

Disposition

What This Means

RETAINED + AUGMENTED

All existing systems of record remain in place. No rip-and-replace. Agents connect via APIs to read data, write updates, trigger workflows, and orchestrate across systems. The systems keep doing what they do well — storing data, processing transactions, managing compliance. What changes is how humans interact with them: through an agent layer rather than direct system-by-system navigation.

ELIMINATED

Spreadsheets and email-based manual workarounds — the shadow IT of HR operations — are systematically eliminated. Headcount trackers, scorecard aggregation sheets, onboarding checklists, compensation modeling workbooks, calibration worksheets, candidate CRMs in Google Sheets, approval tracking via email threads. These exist because systems of record don’t talk to each other; agents make them talk, eliminating the need for humans to be the integration layer.

NEW CAPABILITY

Capabilities that simply did not exist before: (1) Predictive analytics and ML — flight-risk prediction, skills-based matching, real-time pay equity, engagement driver analysis. (2) Agentic orchestration — Gemini Enterprise as a unified interaction layer replacing the fragmented, context-switching, multi-system workflow that defines HR work today.

Gemini Enterprise does not compete with any system in the customer’s current HR technology stack. It sits on top of the existing ecosystem as an orchestration and intelligence layer. Every system of record is retained. What changes is the human’s relationship with those systems: instead of being the integration layer (copying data between systems, coordinating via email, tracking in spreadsheets), the human becomes the decision-maker and relationship-builder while agents handle the orchestration.

The only category that is eliminated is the one that no customer would defend: the spreadsheets, email chains, and manual workarounds that exist precisely because their systems of record do not talk to each other. Agents make them talk. That is the transformation.

2.3 Process Transformation AS IS vs TO BE

See Appendix 1 - Process Transformation AS IS vs TO BE for a comprehensive side-by-side comparison of how every HR process transforms from its current state to an AI-augmented future state with Gemini Enterprise agents. For each process, every activity step is decomposed into: the as-is state (what happens today), the to-be state (how named agents transform the step), expected qualitative impact (what changes in practice), and key KPI metrics (how to measure success without prescribing specific numeric targets, which require organization-specific baselining).

2.4 Phased Transformation Roadmap Phasing

The transformation unfolds in four phases, organized by business value and organizational readiness rather than by technology layer. Each phase builds on the prior: Phase 1 establishes the agent platform and delivers quick wins, Phase 2 extends coverage and deepens integration, Phase 3 introduces predictive and analytical capabilities, and Phase 4 shifts to continuous optimization. A Phase 1 agent may be any implementation layer (L1 or L2) if it addresses a high-priority need with existing connectors; the phasing is driven by business impact, not technical taxonomy.

Phase 1: Foundation

Connect Core Systems & Deploy First-Wave Agents

Dimension

Details

Timeline

Weeks 1–10

Scope & Objectives

This phase establishes the agent platform, connects to the two foundational systems of record (HRIS and ATS), and deploys agents that address the highest-volume, highest-pain processes. It includes both OOTB (L1) agents that require no integration and Agent Designer (L2) agents that leverage existing connectors to core systems. The goal is to deliver visible, measurable value quickly while building organizational familiarity with AI-assisted HR workflows.

Systems Connected

HRIS (primary), ATS (primary), Calendar & Email, Knowledge Base / Intranet

Manual Workarounds Eliminated

Headcount tracker spreadsheets (replaced by real-time HRIS queries); candidate scorecard aggregation sheets; FAQ lookup time for policy questions; manual interview scheduling email chains

Example Agents Deployed

Employee Query Resolution Agent (L2), Policy Q&A Assistant (L1), Interview Scheduling & Coordination Agent (L2), Job Description Generator & Optimizer (L1), Performance Review Narrative Assistant (L1), Requisition Intake & Validation Agent (L2), New Hire Q&A Assistant (L1), Goal Drafting & Alignment Assistant (L1)

Organizational Readiness Prerequisites

Executive sponsorship secured; HRIS and ATS API access provisioned; knowledge base content audited and refreshed; change management communications launched; pilot user group identified; success metrics defined and baselined

Success Criteria

Agent adoption rate among pilot users; query deflection rate for Tier-0 HR service; interview scheduling time reduction; user satisfaction with agent interactions

Phase 2: Expansion

Extend Across Process Domains & Deeper System Integration

Dimension

Details

Timeline

Weeks 8–20

Scope & Objectives

This phase extends agent coverage across more process domains and integrates with the broader HR technology ecosystem. It deploys agents that require multi-system orchestration (connecting payroll, benefits, LMS, IT ticketing, compensation systems) and introduces the first custom agents that require more sophisticated logic. The goal is to reach a tipping point where agents are embedded in most daily HR workflows.

Systems Connected

Payroll, Benefits Platforms, LMS, IT Ticketing, Compensation System, Background Check Vendor, Survey Platform, Recognition Platform, Time & Attendance

Manual Workarounds Eliminated

Onboarding checklist spreadsheets; payroll input validation spreadsheets; compensation modeling workbooks; compliance training tracking spreadsheets; benefits enrollment FAQ email volume; manual pre-boarding coordination emails; recognition tracking spreadsheets

Example Agents Deployed

Onboarding Orchestration Agent (L2), Pre-boarding Orchestration Agent (L2), Payroll Input Validation Agent (L2), Benefits Q&A & Enrollment Assistant (L2), Compliance Tracking & Escalation Agent (L2), Offboarding Orchestration Agent (L2), Offer Package Modeler Agent (L3), Review Cycle Orchestration Agent (L2), Leave & Accommodation Intake Agent (L2), 1:1 Meeting Prep Agent (L2), OKR Progress Tracker Agent (L2)

Organizational Readiness Prerequisites

Phase 1 adoption validated and expanded beyond pilot; API access provisioned for payroll, benefits, LMS, and IT systems; data quality baseline established across connected systems; HR operations team trained on agent-exception workflows; escalation protocols defined for all agent-assisted processes

Success Criteria

Process coverage (% of HR processes with active agents); manual workaround elimination count; cross-system workflow completion rate; HR operations efficiency metrics; employee experience scores for agent-assisted processes

Phase 3: Intelligence

Predictive Capabilities, Advanced Analytics & Skills Intelligence

Dimension

Details

Timeline

Weeks 16–30

Scope & Objectives
