This phase layers in the predictive and analytical capabilities that represent the highest-value, most differentiated transformation outcomes. It requires data infrastructure maturity (unified HR data lake), ML model development (Vertex AI), and organizational readiness to act on predictive insights. Custom ADK agents and Data Agents deploy together — the data layer feeds the intelligence layer. The goal is to shift HR from reactive to predictive and prescriptive.

Systems Connected

HR Data Warehouse/Lake (BigQuery/Snowflake), Financial Planning System, Equity Administration, Case Management (ER), Vertex AI (ML model serving), all Phase 1 & 2 systems feeding the data layer

Manual Workarounds Eliminated

Manual attrition analysis in spreadsheets; ad-hoc compensation modeling; manual calibration worksheet preparation; one-off workforce scenario modeling in PowerPoint; manual pay equity audits via external consultants; manual engagement survey analysis cycles

Example Agents Deployed

Attrition Prediction & Intervention Agent (L3), HR Data Lake Query Agent (L4), Workforce Scenario Modeling Agent (L3), Market Benchmarking Analysis Agent (L3), Pay Equity Audit Agent (L4), Skills Gap Analyzer Agent (L3), ER Case Intelligence Agent (L3), Calibration Analytics Agent (L4), Engagement Insight Synthesizer Agent (L3), Successor Readiness Assessment Agent (L3), Resume Screening & Shortlisting Agent (L3), Restructuring Impact Assessment Agent (L3)

Organizational Readiness Prerequisites

HR data warehouse established with unified schema across HRIS, ATS, LMS, engagement, and compensation data; data governance framework in place; ML model development and validation process defined; ethical AI guidelines established for predictive scoring (transparency, consent, bias monitoring); People Analytics team skilled in model interpretation; HRBP team trained on acting on predictive insights

Success Criteria

Predictive model accuracy and coverage; self-service analytics adoption rate; time from insight to intervention; People Analytics team capacity shift (from reporting to strategic analysis); data quality and freshness across the unified warehouse

Phase 4: Optimization

Continuous Improvement, Scale & Maturity

Dimension

Details

Timeline

Ongoing (from Week 24+)

Scope & Objectives

This phase shifts from deployment to continuous optimization. Agents are refined based on usage data and outcome feedback. ML models are retrained on expanding datasets. New agents are developed for emerging needs. The agent ecosystem becomes self-improving — each interaction generates data that makes the next interaction better. The goal is to establish a sustainable operating model for AI-augmented HR.

Systems Connected

All systems from Phases 1–3; new systems added as the HR tech stack evolves; agent-to-agent orchestration for complex multi-step workflows

Manual Workarounds Eliminated

Remaining manual processes identified through usage analytics; edge cases and exceptions that were initially handled manually brought into agent workflows; custom reporting that was outside the data agent’s initial scope

Example Agents Deployed

All 82 agents in production; new agents developed based on emerging needs; existing agents enhanced with additional capabilities; agent-to-agent handoff patterns refined; Total Rewards Optimizer Agent (L3), HiPo Identification & Nomination Agent (L3), Inclusive Hiring Audit Agent (L3), Workforce Cost Modeling Agent (L4), Engagement-to-Outcome Correlation Agent (L4)

Organizational Readiness Prerequisites

AI-augmented HR is the default operating model, not a project; continuous improvement process established with regular agent performance reviews; feedback loops from agent users to agent development team; model retraining cadence established; organizational change management embedded rather than project-based

Success Criteria

Agent utilization trends (sustained or growing adoption); outcome improvement trends (attrition, time-to-fill, employee experience); model accuracy improvement over time; new agent development velocity; HR team satisfaction with AI-augmented workflows; cost efficiency metrics

2.5 Sample Transformation Scenario

The following scenario illustrates how the phased transformation roadmap applies to a specific customer technology stack. This is a representative mid-large enterprise (5,000–20,000 employees) running a common combination of HR systems. The table shows how each system in their stack is connected and transformed across the four phases.

Organization: Mid-Large Enterprise, 5,000–20,000 employees, multi-geography (US + EMEA + APAC), multiple business units, mixed workforce (corporate + field/frontline)

System

Category

Phase 1: Foundation

Phase 2: Expansion

Phase 3: Intelligence

Phase 4: Optimization

Workday HCM

HRIS

Connect via API; agents read/write employee data, validate requisitions, orchestrate lifecycle transactions

Deeper integration for compensation, benefits enrollment, leave management, payroll input validation

Feed unified data warehouse for predictive analytics and workforce modeling

Continuous optimization of data flows and agent-HRIS interaction patterns

Greenhouse

ATS

Connect via API; agents manage req intake, schedule interviews, track pipeline, send communications

Offer management integration; pre-boarding workflow triggers from offer acceptance

Hiring funnel analytics for DEI audit and sourcing channel optimization

Hiring outcome feedback loops to improve screening and interview question quality

ADP Workforce Now

Payroll

—

Connect for payroll input validation and post-run reconciliation

Feed payroll data into workforce cost modeling

Continuous refinement of validation rules based on error patterns

Cornerstone OnDemand

LMS

—

Connect for compliance training assignment, tracking, and escalation; learning path recommendations

Skills data feeds skills gap analysis and workforce planning models

Learning-to-performance correlation analysis for program optimization

SuccessFactors Performance

Performance Mgmt

Agents generate review narratives and goal drafts (L1, no integration needed)

Connect for OKR tracking, review cycle orchestration, calibration data feeds

Performance data feeds succession planning and HiPo identification models

Feedback trend analysis and calibration bias monitoring

LinkedIn Recruiter + SeekOut

Sourcing

—

Connect for automated candidate sourcing, outreach, and pipeline nurturing

Source-of-hire analytics for channel optimization

Outreach effectiveness optimization based on response data

Qualtrics

Engagement Survey

Agent assists survey design and launch communications (L1)

Connect via API for automated survey triggers and response monitoring

Multi-dimensional analysis, driver identification, engagement-to-outcome correlation

Continuous pulse survey optimization and action plan tracking

ServiceNow

IT Ticketing & HR Service

Connect for automated IT provisioning tickets in onboarding/offboarding

HR service desk integration for query routing and case tracking

Service delivery analytics for continuous improvement

Self-healing workflows based on pattern detection

Google Workspace

Calendar & Email

Connect via API for interview scheduling, automated reminders, and communications

Extended to onboarding scheduling, 1:1 prep, and milestone coordination

—

—

Spreadsheets & Email

Manual Workarounds

Headcount trackers, FAQ lookups, scheduling email chains eliminated

Onboarding checklists, payroll validation sheets, comp modeling workbooks eliminated

Calibration worksheets, scenario models, ad-hoc analysis spreadsheets eliminated

Remaining edge-case workarounds identified and eliminated through usage analytics

No major system investments are replaced. Workday, Greenhouse, ADP, Cornerstone, SuccessFactors, LinkedIn Recruiter, Qualtrics, ServiceNow, and Google Workspace all remain in place and continue to serve their core functions.

What disappears is the manual glue. The spreadsheets, the email chains, the copy-paste workflows, the approval-tracking threads, the reconciliation workbooks. These are eliminated progressively across phases as agents take over the orchestration role that humans currently play between systems.

What appears is intelligence. Predictive attrition models, real-time pay equity analysis, skills-based workforce planning, engagement driver identification — capabilities that most HR organizations aspire to but cannot deliver because their teams are consumed by the operational coordination that agents now handle.

The transformation is additive, not substitutive. It does not ask the customer to change their systems. It asks them to change their operating model: from human-as-integration-layer to agent-as-integration-layer, with humans freed for the judgment, empathy, and relationship work that no agent can replace.

Chapter 3 — HR & PeopleOps Agentic Use Case Map

3.1 Agentic Use Case Framework Overview

This chapter maps every HR process defined in Chapter 1 (Section 1.3) to one or more purpose-built agents that automate, augment, or accelerate specific portions of the process activity sequence. The map is organized hierarchically: Process Domain → Process → Agent(s), with each agent classified into one of four implementation layers based on integration complexity and development approach.

Multiple agents may participate in a single process flow where the task breakdown, UX boundaries, or system integration requirements warrant distinct agentic capabilities. This reflects real-world orchestration patterns where, for example, a data-querying agent feeds insights to a content-generation agent, or a workflow-orchestration agent triggers actions that a separate analytics agent monitors.

3.1.1 Implementation Layer Definitions[a]

Layer

Label

Description

Typical Characteristics

L1

Out-of-the-Box Gemini Enterprise

Leverages native Gemini capabilities: conversational AI, document understanding, content generation, summarization. No custom agent development or 3PP connectors required.

Fastest time-to-value; operates on uploaded documents and user prompts; no system integrations; builds organizational AI familiarity.

L2

Agent Designer + OOTB 3PP Connectors

Purpose-built agents created via Agent Designer that connect to third-party systems (HRIS, ATS, LMS, calendar, email, ticketing) via out-of-the-box data connectors.

Multi-step workflow orchestration; real-time data access; action execution across systems; conversational Gemini interface; configurable without custom code.

L3

Custom Agents (ADK Runtime + GE UI)

Custom agents developed using the Agent Development Kit (ADK) runtime with Gemini Enterprise as the user interaction layer. Involves ML model integration, complex business rules, and multi-system API orchestration.

Highest-value, most differentiated capabilities; requires engineering investment; sophisticated orchestration logic; predictive/prescriptive capabilities; RAG over domain corpora.

L4

Data Agents (Tertiary Priority)

Agents operating at the analytics/data layer, enabling natural-language access to structured HR data in data lakes, warehouses, and analytical platforms. Translate business questions into queries.

Dependent on data infrastructure maturity; NL-to-SQL translation; visualization generation; statistical analysis; data governance integration.

3.1.2 Use Case Map Summary

Metric

Count

Total Process Domains

10

Total Processes Mapped

36

Total Agents Identified

82

Layer 1 – OOTB Agents

23

Layer 2 – Agent Designer Agents

23

Layer 3 – Custom ADK Agents

14

Layer 4 – Data Agents

22

3.2 Consolidated Use Case Map

The following master table presents all agents organized by process domain and process, providing a single-view reference of the entire agentic landscape. Each row represents one agent with its ID, name, implementation layer, and the portion of the process activity sequence it covers (referencing Chapter 1, Section 1.3).

The layer designation is not prescriptive, only a north star subject to in context refinement.

Chapter 4 — HR & PeopleOps Agentic Use Case Deep Dive

This section expands each agent with its full description, including the specific process activities it covers and how it fits within the multi-agent orchestration pattern for its parent process. Each agent entry is designed to feed directly into Chapter 2.3, where detailed use case specifications and discovery questions will be developed.

Process Domain 1 - Workforce Planning & Organizational Design

Process 1.1: Strategic Workforce Planning

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 3 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Workforce Scenario Modeling Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-101

Agent Name

Workforce Scenario Modeling Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 3: Custom ADK

Activity Coverage

Forecast future talent demand → Identify gaps (skills, headcount, location) → Develop supply-side strategies

Description

Ingests business forecasts, HRIS headcount data, attrition models, and labor market signals to generate multi-scenario workforce plans with build/buy/borrow/bot recommendations under varying assumptions.

Labor Market Intelligence Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-102

Agent Name

Labor Market Intelligence Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Analyze business strategy → Forecast future talent demand

Description

Queries external labor market data (salary benchmarks, talent availability, competitor hiring) joined with internal skills inventory to surface talent supply risks by role and geography.

Workforce Plan Document Drafter

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-103

Agent Name

Workforce Plan Document Drafter

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Create workforce plan → Gain executive approval

Description

Generates executive-ready workforce plan narratives, summaries, and presentation content from structured planning inputs and scenario model outputs.

Process 1.2: Organization Design & Restructuring

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 3 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Org Structure Analyzer Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-104

Agent Name

Org Structure Analyzer Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Analyze current org structure → Benchmark spans & layers

Description

Queries org hierarchy data warehouse to compute span-of-control, layers, manager-to-IC ratio, and benchmarks against configurable industry standards.

Change Communication Drafter

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-105

Agent Name

Change Communication Drafter

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Change management plan → Communication cascade

Description

Drafts restructuring communications across multiple stakeholder audiences (affected employees, managers, executives, all-hands) with appropriate tone calibration and FAQ generation.

Restructuring Impact Assessment Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-106

Agent Name

Restructuring Impact Assessment Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 3: Custom ADK

Activity Coverage

Role mapping & job architecture alignment → Impact assessment (RIF, redeployment, reskilling)

Description

Models workforce transition scenarios including RIF lists, redeployment matching based on skills adjacency, reskilling investment estimates, and severance cost projections.

Process 1.3: Job Architecture & Role Management

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Job Description Generator & Optimizer

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-107

Agent Name

Job Description Generator & Optimizer

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Write standardized job descriptions → Map competency profiles per level

Description

Generates inclusive, compliant job descriptions from role requirements, competency profiles, and job architecture taxonomy. Flags biased language and ensures consistency with organizational templates.

Job Architecture Sync Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-108

Agent Name

Job Architecture Sync Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Align with compensation bands → Publish in HRIS

Description

Connects to HRIS and compensation systems to validate job-to-band mappings, identify misalignments, and push approved updates to the system of record.

Process Domain 2 - Talent Acquisition & Onboarding

Process 2.1: Requisition & Approval

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Requisition Intake & Validation Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-201

Agent Name

Requisition Intake & Validation Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Hiring manager submits requisition → HRBP validates against workforce plan → Finance confirms budget

Description

Guides hiring managers through requisition creation via conversational flow, auto-populates from workforce plan, validates budget availability against finance systems, and routes through approval workflow.

Requisition Prioritization Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-202

Agent Name

Requisition Prioritization Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Approvals workflow → Assign recruiter

Description

Analyzes open requisition portfolio against business urgency, time-to-fill benchmarks, and recruiter capacity to recommend prioritization and recruiter assignment.

Process 2.2: Sourcing & Candidate Pipeline

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 3 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Candidate Sourcing & Outreach Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-203

Agent Name

Candidate Sourcing & Outreach Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Proactive outreach (LinkedIn, databases) → Candidate screening (resume/AI) → Nurture passive candidates

Description

Connects to ATS and sourcing platforms to identify matching candidates, generate personalized outreach messages, and manage drip nurture sequences for passive talent pools.

Resume Screening & Shortlisting Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-204

Agent Name

Resume Screening & Shortlisting Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 3: Custom ADK

Activity Coverage

Candidate screening (resume/AI) → Build talent pipeline

Description

Custom agent applying role-specific scoring models to parse, evaluate, and rank applications against job requirements and competency profiles. Includes bias detection and adverse impact monitoring.

Sourcing Channel Analytics Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-205

Agent Name

Sourcing Channel Analytics Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Develop sourcing strategy (channels, events, referrals)

Description

Queries recruiting data warehouse to analyze source-of-hire effectiveness, cost-per-source, diversity yield by channel, and conversion rates to inform sourcing strategy decisions.

Process 2.3: Interview & Selection

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 3 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Interview Question & Scorecard Builder

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-206

Agent Name

Interview Question & Scorecard Builder

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Phone/video screen → Technical/skills assessment → Panel interviews (structured)

Description

Generates structured behavioral and technical interview questions with scoring rubrics aligned to the role competency profile. Ensures jurisdiction-specific compliance (ban-the-box, salary history restrictions).

Interview Scheduling & Coordination Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-207

Agent Name

Interview Scheduling & Coordination Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Panel interviews (structured) → Hiring manager interview → Collect scorecards

Description

Connects to ATS, calendar, and email to automate multi-panel interview scheduling, send candidate prep materials, collect interviewer feedback, and handle rescheduling autonomously.

Selection Debrief Summarizer

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-208

Agent Name

Selection Debrief Summarizer

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Debrief & calibration → Selection decision → Document rationale

Description

Synthesizes interviewer scorecards, feedback themes, and assessment results into a structured debrief summary with recommendation rationale for hiring manager review.

Process 2.4: Offer & Pre-boarding

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Offer Package Modeler Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-209

Agent Name

Offer Package Modeler Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 3: Custom ADK

Activity Coverage

Determine compensation package → Generate offer letter → Approvals

Description

Models total compensation scenarios (base, bonus, equity, sign-on, relocation) against internal equity and market benchmarks. Generates offer letter drafts and routes through approval workflows with pre-populated justifications.

Pre-boarding Orchestration Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-210

Agent Name

Pre-boarding Orchestration Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Initiate background verification → Pre-boarding checklist (IT, facilities, buddy assignment) → Welcome communication

Description

Triggers post-acceptance workflows: initiates background checks, creates IT provisioning tickets, assigns onboarding buddy, sends welcome sequences, and tracks pre-boarding checklist completion.

Process 2.5: Onboarding & Integration

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 3 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Onboarding Orchestration Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-211

Agent Name

Onboarding Orchestration Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Day-1 logistics → Orientation program → HR paperwork & compliance training → Buddy/mentor pairing → Role-specific training plan

Description

Multi-step agent that manages the end-to-end onboarding journey: creates IT tickets, assigns compliance training in LMS, schedules orientation sessions, sends milestone reminders, and escalates overdue items.

New Hire Q&A Assistant

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-212

Agent Name

New Hire Q&A Assistant

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

HR paperwork & compliance training → 30/60/90-day milestones → Manager check-ins

Description

Conversational agent answering new hire questions about policies, benefits enrollment, office logistics, culture, and role expectations during the critical first 90 days.

Onboarding Effectiveness Analyzer

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-213

Agent Name

Onboarding Effectiveness Analyzer

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Onboarding survey → Probation review → Full integration confirmation

Description

Queries onboarding completion data, new-hire survey scores, time-to-productivity metrics, and early attrition rates to identify program bottlenecks and generate improvement recommendations.

Process Domain 3 - Performance & Talent Management

Process 3.1: Goal Setting & OKR Alignment

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Goal Drafting & Alignment Assistant

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-301

Agent Name

Goal Drafting & Alignment Assistant

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Manager-employee goal discussion → Draft individual goals/OKRs → Align with team & department goals

Description

Helps managers and employees draft SMART goals and OKRs from company objectives, suggests alignment connections to department/team goals, and flags misalignment or duplication.

OKR Progress Tracker Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-302

Agent Name

OKR Progress Tracker Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Manager approval → Publish in performance system → Quarterly check-ins & recalibration

Description

Connects to performance management system to track OKR progress, send check-in reminders, surface stalled objectives, and generate quarterly alignment reports for managers.

Process 3.2: Continuous Feedback & Check-ins

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

1:1 Meeting Prep Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-303

Agent Name

1:1 Meeting Prep Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Schedule regular 1:1s → Prepare talking points → Discuss progress, blockers, development

Description

Connects to calendar, performance system, and feedback platforms to auto-generate personalized 1:1 agendas with goal progress, recent feedback themes, and suggested coaching topics.

Feedback Trend Analyzer

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-304

Agent Name

Feedback Trend Analyzer

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Aggregate feedback trends → Input to performance review

Description

Analyzes feedback text across peer reviews, manager notes, and recognition data to identify sentiment trends, strengths/development themes, and potential performance signals at team and individual level.

Process 3.3: Annual/Semi-Annual Performance Review

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 3 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Performance Review Narrative Assistant

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-305

Agent Name

Performance Review Narrative Assistant

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Self-assessment → Manager assessment → 360-degree feedback

Description

Transforms bullet-point observations into well-structured, balanced review narratives aligned to rating definitions. Flags potentially biased or legally risky language.

Calibration Analytics Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-306

Agent Name

Calibration Analytics Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Calibration sessions → Final rating assignment

Description

Surfaces pre-calibration rating distributions by manager, team, demographics, and historical patterns. Identifies statistical outliers and potential bias indicators to facilitate evidence-based calibration discussions.

Review Cycle Orchestration Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-307

Agent Name

Review Cycle Orchestration Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Launch review cycle → Document & sign-off → Appeal process

Description

Manages the end-to-end review cycle workflow: sends launch communications, tracks completion rates, escalates overdue submissions, and triggers post-review actions (compensation link, development plan creation).

Process 3.4: Succession Planning

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Successor Readiness Assessment Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-308

Agent Name

Successor Readiness Assessment Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 3: Custom ADK

Activity Coverage

Assess bench strength → Evaluate potential (9-box) → Identify successors

Description

Integrates performance history, competency assessments, 360 feedback, leadership development completion, and career trajectory data to generate data-driven 9-box placements and successor readiness scores.

Succession Pipeline Dashboard Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-309

Agent Name

Succession Pipeline Dashboard Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Identify critical roles → Executive review & validation → Monitor progress

Description

Queries succession data to compute bench strength ratios, pipeline coverage for critical roles, successor development plan progress, and risk flags (single points of failure, retirement eligibility).

Process 3.5: High-Potential (HiPo) Program Management

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

HiPo Identification & Nomination Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-310

Agent Name

HiPo Identification & Nomination Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 3: Custom ADK

Activity Coverage

Define HiPo criteria → Nomination & assessment → Selection committee review

Description

Applies configurable HiPo criteria across performance, potential, and aspiration dimensions using multi-source data (reviews, assessments, manager nominations). Generates candidate profiles for selection committee review.

HiPo Development Journey Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-311

Agent Name

HiPo Development Journey Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Design accelerated development → Track progress → Graduate to successor pools

Description

Connects to LMS, mentoring platform, and project management tools to orchestrate individualized development journeys: assigns stretch rotations, schedules executive exposure sessions, and tracks milestone completion.

Process Domain 4 - Compensation, Benefits & Total Rewards

Process 4.1: Compensation Benchmarking & Band Design

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Market Benchmarking Analysis Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-401

Agent Name

Market Benchmarking Analysis Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 3: Custom ADK

Activity Coverage

Select survey sources → Participate in compensation surveys → Analyze market data by role/geo → Compa-ratio analysis

Description

Integrates external survey data (Mercer, Radford, Payscale) with internal compensation data to perform on-demand benchmarking by role, level, and geography. Computes compa-ratios and flags out-of-band positions.

Compensation Philosophy Communicator

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-402

Agent Name

Compensation Philosophy Communicator

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Define compensation philosophy → Executive approval → Communicate to managers

Description

Generates compensation philosophy documentation, manager talking points, and employee-facing FAQ content explaining pay structures, market positioning, and total rewards approach.

Process 4.2: Annual Compensation Review (Merit & Promotion)

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 3 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Merit & Promotion Budget Modeler Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-403

Agent Name

Merit & Promotion Budget Modeler Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 3: Custom ADK

Activity Coverage

Budget allocation from finance → Manager recommendations → HR review for equity & consistency → Calibration sessions

Description

Models merit increase scenarios within budget constraints, applies performance-tier differentiation rules, flags equity inconsistencies, and generates manager recommendation worksheets with guardrails.

Pay Equity Audit Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-404

Agent Name

Pay Equity Audit Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Pay equity analysis → Final approvals

Description

Runs multi-variate regression on compensation data controlling for legitimate factors (tenure, performance, location, role) to identify statistically significant pay gaps by gender, ethnicity, and other protected categories.

Compensation Letter Generator

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-405

Agent Name

Compensation Letter Generator

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Generate compensation letters → Manager delivery conversations

Description

Produces personalized compensation adjustment letters and total rewards statements from approved review data, including manager talking-point guides for delivery conversations.

Process 4.3: Benefits Administration

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Benefits Q&A & Enrollment Assistant

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-406

Agent Name

Benefits Q&A & Enrollment Assistant

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Open enrollment campaign → Employee enrollment → Life event changes → Claims support

Description

Connects to HRIS and benefits platforms to answer employee benefits questions, guide enrollment selections with plan comparison, process life-event changes, and route claims issues to appropriate carriers.

Benefits Utilization & Cost Analyzer

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-407

Agent Name

Benefits Utilization & Cost Analyzer

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Utilization monitoring → Annual renewal

Description

Queries benefits data warehouse to analyze plan utilization patterns, cost trends, employee satisfaction by plan type, and generates data-driven recommendations for renewal negotiations and plan design changes.

Process 4.4: Equity & Long-Term Incentive Management

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Total Rewards Optimizer Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-408

Agent Name

Total Rewards Optimizer Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 3: Custom ADK

Activity Coverage

Design equity plan → Grant allocation → Vesting schedule management → Exercise/settlement processing

Description

Models total compensation scenarios for individual offers, retention packages, or annual review cycles. Factors in internal equity, external benchmarks, budget constraints, and cross-jurisdiction tax optimization. For executives, models equity dilution and LTI scenarios.

Equity Participant Communicator

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-409

Agent Name

Equity Participant Communicator

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Participant communication → Tax withholding

Description

Generates clear, personalized equity grant communications, vesting schedule explanations, and tax impact summaries for program participants.

Process Domain 5 - Learning & Development

Process 5.1: Training Needs Analysis

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Skills Gap Analyzer Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-501

Agent Name

Skills Gap Analyzer Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 3: Custom ADK

Activity Coverage

Gather inputs → Analyze competency gaps → Prioritize learning needs

Description

Maps organizational skills taxonomy from job descriptions, project assignments, learning completions, and self-assessments. Identifies gaps against target-state competency requirements and prioritizes learning investments by business impact.

L&D Plan Narrative Drafter

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-502

Agent Name

L&D Plan Narrative Drafter

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Create annual L&D plan → Stakeholder sign-off

Description

Transforms skills-gap analysis outputs and priority recommendations into a structured annual L&D plan document with budget justifications, program descriptions, and success metrics for stakeholder approval.

Process 5.2: Learning Content Development & Curation

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Learning Content Summarizer & Quiz Generator

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-503

Agent Name

Learning Content Summarizer & Quiz Generator

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Develop in-house content → Curate external content → Quality review

Description

Ingests training materials (documents, transcripts, slide decks) and generates microlearning summaries, quiz questions, flashcards, and metadata tags for LMS cataloging.

Learning Path Recommendation Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-504

Agent Name

Learning Path Recommendation Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Publish in LMS/LXP → Assign to learning paths

Description

Based on an employee’s role, career aspiration, skills assessment, and performance feedback, recommends personalized learning paths from the LMS catalog and external content libraries.

Process 5.3: Compliance & Mandatory Training

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Compliance Training Content Generator

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-505

Agent Name

Compliance Training Content Generator

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Identify regulatory requirements by jurisdiction → Map to training modules

Description

Generates jurisdiction-specific compliance training content drafts (harassment prevention, data privacy, safety) that L&D teams review and publish. Adapts content for regulatory updates.

Compliance Tracking & Escalation Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-506

Agent Name

Compliance Tracking & Escalation Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Assign to applicable populations → Set deadlines → Track completion → Send reminders & escalations

Description

Connects to LMS to monitor compliance training completion, send tiered reminders (employee, manager, VP), auto-escalate overdue items, and generate audit-ready compliance certification reports.

Process 5.4: Leadership Development Programs

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Leadership Program Design Assistant

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-507

Agent Name

Leadership Program Design Assistant

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Design program curriculum → Select facilitators/coaches → Participant nomination & selection

Description

Assists in curriculum design by generating session outlines, facilitator briefs, and participant assessment frameworks. Helps draft nomination criteria and communication materials.

Program Impact Evaluation Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-508

Agent Name

Program Impact Evaluation Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Evaluate outcomes (Kirkpatrick model) → Alumni network

Description

Analyzes multi-level program effectiveness data: satisfaction scores (L1), knowledge assessments (L2), behavioral change indicators (L3), and business impact metrics (L4, e.g., promotion rates, engagement lift of graduates).

Process Domain 6 - Employee Relations & Compliance

Process 6.1: Grievance & Complaint Management

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

ER Case Intelligence Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-601

Agent Name

ER Case Intelligence Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 3: Custom ADK

Activity Coverage

Intake & categorize → Assign case owner → Preliminary assessment → Investigation

Description

Auto-classifies incoming ER cases by severity and type, checks for patterns (repeat complaints, same manager/department), recommends investigation approaches based on case precedents, and generates investigation summary drafts. Maintains secure audit trail.

ER Case Analytics Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-602

Agent Name

ER Case Analytics Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Monitor for retaliation → Close case & document

Description

Queries case management data to surface trends (volume by category, resolution times, repeat offender patterns), generates monthly ER metrics reports, and flags post-resolution retaliation risk indicators.

Process 6.2: Disciplinary Action & Performance Improvement

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

PIP Documentation Assistant

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-603

Agent Name

PIP Documentation Assistant

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) → Monitor PIP progress → PIP outcome decision

Description

Generates structured PIP documents from manager inputs, ensures inclusion of required legal elements, creates milestone tracking templates, and drafts outcome communication for both successful and unsuccessful PIP conclusions.

Progressive Discipline Advisor Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-604

Agent Name

Progressive Discipline Advisor Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Identify performance/conduct issue → Informal coaching → Verbal warning → Written warning

Description

Guides managers through the progressive discipline process via conversational flow, checking policy compliance at each step, flagging required documentation, and connecting to HRIS to log disciplinary actions.

Process 6.3: Policy Development & Management

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Policy Q&A Assistant

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-605

Agent Name

Policy Q&A Assistant

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Communication & training → Publish in policy repository

Description

Answers natural-language employee and HRBP questions about HR policies (leave, benefits, conduct, remote work) by searching the policy document corpus and providing instant, cited answers with source references.

Policy Drafting & Review Assistant

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-606

Agent Name

Policy Drafting & Review Assistant

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Identify policy need → Draft policy → Legal review

Description

Generates initial policy drafts from requirements and regulatory guidance, produces red-line comparisons against existing policies, and creates employee-facing policy summaries and training FAQ content.

Process 6.4: Workplace Accommodations & Leave Management

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Leave & Accommodation Intake Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-607

Agent Name

Leave & Accommodation Intake Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Employee requests accommodation/leave → Gather documentation → Assess eligibility

Description

Guides employees through leave requests or accommodation applications via conversational flow. Collects required documentation, checks eligibility against policy rules and HRIS data, and routes for approval with pre-populated forms.

Leave Utilization & Compliance Analyzer

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-608

Agent Name

Leave Utilization & Compliance Analyzer

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Track leave balances → Return-to-work process → Ongoing monitoring

Description

Queries leave data to analyze utilization by type, identify compliance risks (approaching FMLA exhaustion, ADA tracking gaps), monitor return-to-work timelines, and generate regulatory compliance reports.

Process Domain 7 - Employee Engagement & Culture

Process 7.1: Employee Engagement Surveys

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 3 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Survey Design & Communication Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-701

Agent Name

Survey Design & Communication Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Design survey instrument → Communicate purpose & confidentiality → Launch survey

Description

Assists in survey question design, generates launch communications emphasizing confidentiality and purpose, and creates multi-channel distribution content (email, Slack, intranet).

Engagement Insight Synthesizer AgentA-207

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-702

Agent Name

Engagement Insight Synthesizer Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 3: Custom ADK

Activity Coverage

Analyze results → Share results with leaders → Cascade to teams → Action planning workshops

Description

Performs multi-dimensional survey analysis (overall, by BU, demographics, tenure), identifies statistically significant drivers, generates leadership reports with narrative insights, and produces team-level action planning guides.

Engagement-to-Outcome Correlation Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-703

Agent Name

Engagement-to-Outcome Correlation Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Track action plan execution → Pulse surveys for follow-up

Description

Joins engagement scores with business outcomes (productivity, quality, customer NPS, revenue per employee) to identify which engagement drivers have the strongest causal impact. Measures action plan effectiveness over time.

Process 7.2: Recognition & Rewards Programs

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Recognition Program Analytics Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-704

Agent Name

Recognition Program Analytics Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Monitor adoption → Budget management → Program effectiveness review

Description

Analyzes recognition frequency, distribution equity (by team, level, demographics), budget utilization, and correlates recognition patterns with engagement scores and retention outcomes.

Recognition Nudge & Celebration Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-705

Agent Name

Recognition Nudge & Celebration Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Launch & communicate → Monitor adoption

Description

Connects to HRIS and recognition platform to send proactive nudges to managers with low recognition activity, auto-generate milestone celebration messages (work anniversaries, project completions), and surface recognition opportunities.

Process 7.3: Internal Communications & Culture Initiatives

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Employee Communication Drafter

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-706

Agent Name

Employee Communication Drafter

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Develop communication strategy → Content calendar creation → Town halls & all-hands planning → Intranet/newsletter content

Description

Drafts employee communications across formats (all-hands scripts, newsletter articles, policy announcements, org change notices) with tone calibration, multi-audience variants, and translation support for global workforces.

Communication Reach & Sentiment Analyzer

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-707

Agent Name

Communication Reach & Sentiment Analyzer

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Measure reach & sentiment → Feedback loop

Description

Queries communication analytics data (open rates, intranet page views, town hall attendance, comment sentiment) to measure effectiveness and recommend content optimization strategies.

Process Domain 8 - HR Operations & Shared Services

Process 8.1: Employee Data Management (HRIS)

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

HRIS Data Quality Monitor Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-801

Agent Name

HRIS Data Quality Monitor Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Ongoing data maintenance → Data validation & audits → Integration management

Description

Connects to HRIS to run automated data quality checks (missing fields, invalid entries, stale records), monitor integration sync health, flag exceptions, and generate data quality scorecards.

Employee Data Change Orchestrator

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-802

Agent Name

Employee Data Change Orchestrator

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

New hire data entry → Ongoing data maintenance (promotions, transfers, personal changes)

Description

Processes employee lifecycle transactions by connecting to HRIS APIs: validates inputs, applies business rules, triggers downstream integrations (payroll, benefits, access management), and maintains audit trails.

Process 8.2: HR Service Delivery (Tier 0/1/2)

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Employee Query Resolution Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-803

Agent Name

Employee Query Resolution Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Employee submits query → Tier-0 self-service resolution → Tier-1 generalist handling

Description

Handles employee HR queries by searching the knowledge base, looking up employee-specific data in HRIS (leave balance, benefits elections, pay stubs), and either resolving directly or routing to the appropriate specialist with full context.

Service Delivery Analytics Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-804

Agent Name

Service Delivery Analytics Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Case tracking & SLA monitoring → Knowledge base maintenance → Continuous improvement

Description

Queries HR service desk data to analyze case volumes by category, resolution times, first-contact resolution rates, SLA adherence, CSAT scores, and identifies knowledge base gaps driving repeat queries.

Process 8.3: Payroll Processing

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Payroll Input Validation Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-805

Agent Name

Payroll Input Validation Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Collect time & attendance data → Validate inputs (new hires, changes, terminations) → Apply deductions

Description

Connects to time & attendance, HRIS, and benefits systems to cross-validate payroll inputs, flag discrepancies (missing timesheets, unprocessed changes, garnishment conflicts), and generate pre-payroll exception reports.

Payroll Reconciliation & Compliance Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-806

Agent Name

Payroll Reconciliation & Compliance Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Quality checks → GL posting → Tax filings & remittances → Year-end processing

Description

Queries payroll and finance data to perform post-run reconciliation (register to GL, tax liability verification, benefit deduction matching), identifies variances, and generates compliance-ready tax filing summaries.

Process 8.4: Offboarding & Exit Management

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 3 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

Offboarding Orchestration Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-807

Agent Name

Offboarding Orchestration Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Resignation/termination notification → Knowledge transfer plan → Asset recovery → System access revocation → Benefits continuation (COBRA)

Description

Triggers and manages offboarding workflows upon separation notification: schedules exit interview, creates IT deprovisioning tickets, initiates asset recovery, calculates final pay, and sends COBRA notices.

Exit Interview Insight Synthesizer

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-808

Agent Name

Exit Interview Insight Synthesizer

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Exit interview → Alumni network enrollment → Compliance documentation

Description

Analyzes batch exit interview transcripts to identify recurring themes, sentiment patterns, and actionable recommendations grouped by department, manager, tenure band, or exit reason.

Attrition Analytics Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-809

Agent Name

Attrition Analytics Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Exit interview → Compliance documentation

Description

Queries separation data warehouse to analyze attrition patterns (regrettable vs. non-regrettable, voluntary vs. involuntary), compute time-to-backfill, correlate with engagement data, and generate predictive early-warning indicators.

Process Domain 9 - DEI & Belonging

Process 9.1: DEI Strategy & Metrics

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 3 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

DEI Dashboard & Reporting Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-901

Agent Name

DEI Dashboard & Reporting Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Assess current state → Track metrics → Report to leadership & externally

Description

Queries workforce demographics, hiring funnel diversity, promotion equity, inclusion index, and pay-gap data to generate comprehensive DEI dashboards, board-ready reports, and external reporting submissions (EEO-1, DEI disclosures).

Inclusive Hiring Audit Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-902

Agent Name

Inclusive Hiring Audit Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 3: Custom ADK

Activity Coverage

Set DEI goals → Execute programs → Iterate

Description

Analyzes the hiring funnel at each stage (application, screen, interview, offer, accept) by demographic dimensions to identify where diversity drop-off occurs. Recommends targeted interventions and tracks improvement over time.

DEI Communication & Programming Assistant

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-903

Agent Name

DEI Communication & Programming Assistant

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Develop strategy & initiatives → Secure executive sponsorship → Allocate budget

Description

Generates DEI strategy narratives, initiative proposals with budget justifications, executive sponsorship briefings, and internal programming content for cultural awareness events and heritage celebrations.

Process 9.2: ERG (Employee Resource Group) Management

ERG Engagement & Impact Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-904

Agent Name

ERG Engagement & Impact Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Event programming → Membership engagement → Business impact measurement → Executive sponsor alignment

Description

Connects to event platforms and HRIS to track ERG membership, event attendance, budget utilization, and member engagement. Generates impact reports linking ERG participation to retention and engagement outcomes for executive sponsors.

Process Domain 10 - People Analytics & HR Technology

Process 10.1: Workforce Analytics & Reporting

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 3 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

HR Data Lake Query Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-1001

Agent Name

HR Data Lake Query Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Build data pipelines → Create dashboards & scorecards → Scheduled reporting → Ad-hoc analysis requests

Description

Enables People Analytics analysts and HRBPs to query the unified HR data lake using natural language. Translates questions into SQL/BigQuery queries, executes them, and presents results with visualizations. Handles complex joins across HRIS, ATS, LMS, and survey datasets.

Attrition Prediction & Intervention Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-1002

Agent Name

Attrition Prediction & Intervention Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 3: Custom ADK

Activity Coverage

Predictive modeling (attrition, performance) → Insights presentation → Action recommendations

Description

Ingests HRIS signals (tenure, compensation history, promotion velocity), engagement surveys, manager feedback, and external labor market data. Runs ML models to generate flight-risk scores, triggers HRBP alerts, suggests retention actions, and drafts stay-interview talking points.

Workforce Cost Modeling Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-1003

Agent Name

Workforce Cost Modeling Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 4: Data Agent

Activity Coverage

Scheduled reporting → Ad-hoc analysis requests

Description

Queries financial and HR data to model fully-loaded workforce costs under different scenarios (hiring plans, location shifts, contractor-to-FTE conversions, benefits changes). Produces executive-ready cost comparisons with drill-down capability.

Process 10.2: HR Technology Governance

Multi-Agent Process: This process is served by 2 agents operating on distinct portions of the activity sequence. Agents are listed in approximate activity-sequence order.

HR Tech Stack Intelligence Agent

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-1004

Agent Name

HR Tech Stack Intelligence Agent

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 2: Agent Designer

Activity Coverage

Maintain HR tech landscape inventory → License optimization → Contract renewals

Description

Connects to IT asset management and vendor platforms to maintain real-time HR technology inventory, track license utilization, flag underused tools, and generate renewal timeline alerts with cost optimization recommendations.

Vendor Evaluation Assistant

Attribute

Details

Agent ID

A-1005

Agent Name

Vendor Evaluation Assistant

Impl. Layer Archetype

Layer 1: OOTB

Activity Coverage

Evaluate new tool requests → Vendor assessment & selection

Description

Assists in structuring RFP documents, generating evaluation scorecards from demo notes, and creating comparative analysis summaries across vendor options based on configurable criteria (features, integration, cost, compliance).

Agent Discovery Questionnaire

1. Workforce Planning & Organizational Design

ID

Agent Name

Layer

Process

Discovery Areas

▶ Strategic Workforce Planning

A-101

Workforce Scenario Modeling Agent

L3: Custom ADK

Strategic Workforce Planning

1. What business planning inputs (revenue forecasts, project pipelines, expansion plans) are available via API or structured feeds to drive scenario assumptions?

2. What is the current workforce planning maturity – spreadsheet-based, tool-assisted, or connected to financial planning systems (Anaplan, Adaptive)?

3. How many scenarios does leadership typically evaluate, and what are the key assumption variables (attrition rate, hiring ramp, contractor mix)?

4. What confidence intervals or sensitivity analyses are expected in scenario outputs to communicate uncertainty?

5. Who are the plan consumers (CHRO, CFO, BU heads), and what output format do they need (dashboard, exportable model, presentation)?

A-102

Labor Market Intelligence Agent

L4: Data Agent

Strategic Workforce Planning

1. What external labor market data sources does the organization subscribe to (Lightcast/EMSI, LinkedIn Talent Insights, Burning Glass)?

2. What internal skills inventory or taxonomy exists that can be mapped to external labor market classifications?

3. What are the critical roles or geographies where talent supply risk is most acute and needs proactive monitoring?

4. How frequently should labor market intelligence be refreshed – real-time, monthly, or on-demand for planning cycles?

5. What data infrastructure exists to join external market data with internal HRIS headcount and skills data?

A-103

Workforce Plan Document Drafter

L1: OOTB

Strategic Workforce Planning

1. What are the standard templates, formats, and narrative structures expected for workforce plan documents and board presentations?

2. Who reviews and approves workforce plan documents, and what is the typical iteration cycle?

3. What level of detail is expected – executive summary only, or granular BU-level appendices?

4. Are there existing exemplar workforce plans that can train the agent on tone and structure expectations?

5. How should the agent handle confidential workforce planning information (RIF scenarios, M&A plans) in generated documents?

▶ Organization Design & Restructuring

A-104

Org Structure Analyzer Agent

L4: Data Agent

Organization Design & Restructuring

1. How is organizational hierarchy data currently stored – in HRIS, a separate org charting tool, or spreadsheets?

2. What span-of-control and layers benchmarks does the organization use, and are industry-specific comparators needed?

3. What granularity is required – enterprise-wide, BU-level, or down to individual manager spans?

4. How frequently are org structure analyses triggered (M&A, annual planning, efficiency reviews)?

5. What visualization or export formats are needed for leadership consumption?

A-105

Change Communication Drafter

L1: OOTB

Organization Design & Restructuring

1. What restructuring communication types are typically needed (employee notification, manager briefing, all-hands script, FAQ, press statement)?

2. Does the organization have established tone-of-voice guidelines and legal review requirements for restructuring communications?

3. How many stakeholder audiences must be addressed, and what are the timing/sequencing constraints for cascade communications?

4. What languages and locales need support for global restructurings?

5. How will confidentiality be maintained for sensitive draft communications prior to announcement?

A-106

Restructuring Impact Assessment Agent

L3: Custom ADK

Organization Design & Restructuring

1. What employee data attributes are available for skills adjacency matching (competencies, certifications, project history, learning completions)?

2. How are redeployment matching criteria defined – role-based, skills-based, location-based, or a combination?

3. What severance calculation rules apply across jurisdictions, and where is this logic maintained?

4. What reskilling cost models or vendor partnerships exist that should be factored into transition scenario costing?

5. What legal review and human-in-the-loop safeguards are required before AI-generated RIF lists or redeployment recommendations are acted upon?

▶ Job Architecture & Role Management

A-107

Job Description Generator & Optimizer

L1: OOTB

Job Architecture & Role Management

1. Does the organization have a formalized job architecture with standardized job families, levels, and competency profiles?

2. What inclusive language and compliance standards (OFCCP, EEOC, local regulations) must generated JDs adhere to?

3. How many JDs are created or updated annually, and what is the current turnaround time?

4. Are there existing JD templates or brand voice style guides for consistency?

5. Who approves published JDs, and what is the review workflow before posting to career sites?

A-108

Job Architecture Sync Agent

L2: Agent Dsgn

Job Architecture & Role Management

1. What HRIS and compensation systems hold the job code master and salary band tables, and do they support API-based updates?

2. How frequently do job-to-band mappings change, and what is the current manual process for resolving misalignments?

3. What approval workflow is required before the agent can push changes to production HRIS data?

4. How are new job codes provisioned – governance committee or delegated authority?

5. What downstream systems (ATS, payroll, finance) are affected by job architecture changes?

 

2. Talent Acquisition & Onboarding

ID

Agent Name

Layer

Process

Discovery Areas

▶ Requisition & Approval

A-201

Requisition Intake & Validation Agent

L2: Agent Dsgn

Requisition & Approval

1. What ATS is in use, and does it support API-based requisition creation, status updates, and approval workflow triggers?

2. How is the workforce plan connected to requisition approvals – manual validation or system-enforced headcount controls?

3. What financial system holds budget data, and can the agent validate budget availability in real-time?

4. What data does the hiring manager need to provide at requisition creation, and how much can be auto-populated?

5. What are the approval chain rules (by level, cost center, role type), and how often do they change?

A-202

Requisition Prioritization Agent

L4: Data Agent

Requisition & Approval

1. What data signals should drive requisition prioritization (revenue impact, project deadlines, time-open, hiring manager seniority)?

2. How is recruiter capacity currently tracked – workload management system or managed informally?

3. What historical time-to-fill benchmarks exist by role family, level, and geography?

4. How should the agent handle conflicting priorities between BUs competing for recruiting resources?

5. What is the desired output – ranked priority list, recruiter assignment recommendations, or both?

▶ Sourcing & Candidate Pipeline

A-203

Candidate Sourcing & Outreach Agent

L2: Agent Dsgn

Sourcing & Candidate Pipeline

1. What sourcing platforms are in the tech stack (LinkedIn Recruiter, SeekOut, HireEZ), and what API/integration capabilities do they offer?

2. How are outreach templates managed today, and what personalization level is expected?

3. What compliance requirements govern candidate outreach (GDPR consent, CAN-SPAM, CCPA)?

4. How should the agent handle diversity sourcing goals – specific slate diversity requirements by role or BU?

5. What referral program structure exists, and can the agent access referral submission data via ATS?

A-204

Resume Screening & Shortlisting Agent

L3: Custom ADK

Sourcing & Candidate Pipeline

1. What resume parsing infrastructure exists, and what formats need support (PDF, Word, LinkedIn profile imports)?

2. How are role-specific scoring criteria defined – per requisition by hiring managers, or standardized by job family?

3. What bias detection and adverse impact monitoring requirements must the screening model satisfy?

4. What is the desired human-in-the-loop touchpoint – auto-advance candidates or present a ranked shortlist for review?

5. How will screening accuracy be measured and improved (hire-quality correlation, reject-reason feedback loops)?

A-205

Sourcing Channel Analytics Agent

L4: Data Agent

Sourcing & Candidate Pipeline

1. What recruiting data warehouse exists, and does it integrate ATS, sourcing platforms, career site, and referral data?

2. What sourcing effectiveness metrics are tracked today (source-of-hire, cost-per-source, quality-of-hire by source)?

3. How granular should channel analysis be – by job family, level, geography, or diversity dimension?

4. What is the current lag between data availability and actionable analysis?

5. How will the agent handle multi-touch attribution when candidates interact with multiple channels?

▶ Interview & Selection

A-206

Interview Question & Scorecard Builder

L1: OOTB

Interview & Selection

1. Does the organization use structured interviewing, and are there existing competency-to-question mappings?

2. What jurisdiction-specific legal constraints exist (ban-the-box, salary history bans) that vary by hiring location?

3. How will generated scorecards integrate with the ATS evaluation workflow – export, API push, or standalone?

4. What feedback mechanism ensures continuous improvement based on hiring outcome correlations?

5. Are there role-family-specific technical assessment needs requiring specialized question generation?

A-207

Interview Scheduling & Coordination Agent

L2: Agent Dsgn

Interview & Selection

1. What calendar and email systems are in use, and do they support API-based scheduling and automated communications?

2. What is the current average time spent per recruiter on scheduling coordination per week?

3. How should the agent handle multi-timezone, multi-location panel coordination and interviewer conflicts?

4. What escalation rules apply for candidate no-shows, interviewer cancellations, or scheduling conflicts?

5. What candidate communication templates and branding standards must the agent adhere to?

A-208

Selection Debrief Summarizer

L1: OOTB

Interview & Selection

1. How are interviewer scorecards currently collected and aggregated – ATS, email, or shared documents?

2. What debrief format do hiring managers expect (structured report, bullet summary, narrative)?

3. How should the agent handle divergent interviewer opinions or scoring discrepancies?

4. What confidentiality controls are needed for debrief summaries containing candidate evaluations?

5. Should summaries include a recommendation or remain neutral for unbiased committee deliberation?

▶ Offer & Pre-boarding

A-209

Offer Package Modeler Agent

L3: Custom ADK

Offer & Pre-boarding

1. What systems hold compensation bands, equity grant guidelines, and benefits cost information for modeling?

2. What modeling scenarios are most common (standard offer, retention counter-offer, executive package, international hire)?

3. How are internal equity checks currently performed – manual comparison or system-assisted?

4. What approval workflow and delegation-of-authority rules govern offer approvals by package value?

5. How should the agent handle multi-currency and cross-jurisdiction tax implications?

A-210

Pre-boarding Orchestration Agent

L2: Agent Dsgn

Offer & Pre-boarding

1. What systems need orchestration for pre-boarding (HRIS, IT ticketing, facilities, background check vendor, LMS)?

2. What is the current pre-boarding task completion rate, and where are the most common delays?

3. How should the agent handle exceptions (delayed start dates, visa-dependent international hires, remote-first setups)?

4. What buddy/mentor assignment process exists, and what matching criteria should the agent use?

5. What welcome communication templates and branding standards exist?

▶ Onboarding & Integration

A-211

Onboarding Orchestration Agent

L2: Agent Dsgn

Onboarding & Integration

1. Is there a standardized onboarding checklist per role type/location, or does it vary significantly across BUs?

2. What systems need integration (HRIS, IT ticketing, LMS, facilities, payroll), and what are their API capabilities?

3. What are the current onboarding task completion rates, and where are the most common drop-offs?

4. How should the agent handle role-specific variations (engineering setup vs. sales enablement vs. executive onboarding)?

5. What SLAs exist for onboarding tasks (e.g., IT equipment within 24 hours, compliance training within 7 days)?

A-212

New Hire Q&A Assistant

L1: OOTB

Onboarding & Integration

1. What knowledge corpus will the agent draw from – employee handbook, benefits guides, office wiki, department FAQs?

2. What are the most common new-hire questions in the first 90 days, and what percentage are answerable from existing docs?

3. How should the agent escalate questions it cannot answer – to HR Ops, hiring manager, or assigned buddy?

4. What personalization is expected (location-specific info, role-specific resources, benefits enrollment guidance)?

5. How will content accuracy be maintained as policies and programs change?

A-213

Onboarding Effectiveness Analyzer

L4: Data Agent

Onboarding & Integration

1. What onboarding metrics are tracked today (completion rate, time-to-productivity, new-hire satisfaction, early attrition)?

2. How is time-to-productivity measured – manager assessment, system activity, or performance milestone?

3. What data sources need joining for analysis (HRIS, LMS, survey platform, performance system)?

4. What granularity is needed (enterprise-wide, by BU, by role family, by hiring source)?

5. How frequently should reports be generated, and who are the consumers?

 3. Performance & Talent Management

ID

Agent Name

Layer

Process

Discovery Areas

▶ Goal Setting & OKR Alignment

A-301

Goal Drafting & Alignment Assistant

L1: OOTB

Goal Setting & OKR Alignment

1. What goal-setting methodology is used (OKRs, SMART goals, MBOs), and how mature is adoption?

2. How are company and department objectives cascaded and made accessible for employee alignment?

3. What are the common quality issues with employee-written goals (vague, unmeasurable, misaligned)?

4. How should the agent validate alignment – suggest connections, flag conflicts, or both?

5. What performance system stores goals, and can generated content be exported or pasted into it?

A-302

OKR Progress Tracker Agent

L2: Agent Dsgn

Goal Setting & OKR Alignment

1. What performance management system is in use, and does it support API-based goal progress updates?

2. How frequently are check-ins expected (weekly, bi-weekly, monthly), and who initiates them?

3. What constitutes a “stalled” objective that should trigger an alert, and who should be notified?

4. How should the agent handle mid-cycle goal changes or reprioritizations?

5. What reporting format do managers need for quarterly alignment reviews?

▶ Continuous Feedback & Check-ins

A-303

1:1 Meeting Prep Agent

L2: Agent Dsgn

Continuous Feedback & Check-ins

1. What systems hold data for agenda generation (calendar, performance system, feedback platform, recognition tool)?

2. What is the current 1:1 cadence and completion rate across the organization?

3. What talking-point categories should the agenda cover (goal progress, feedback themes, development, career, well-being)?

4. How should the agent handle sensitive feedback themes that should be surfaced carefully?

5. What format should prepared agendas take – email, calendar note, in-app notification, or Slack?

A-304

Feedback Trend Analyzer

L4: Data Agent

Continuous Feedback & Check-ins

1. What feedback data sources exist (peer reviews, manager notes, recognition posts, 360 feedback, pulse comments)?

2. How is feedback text currently analyzed – manually by HRBPs, or with NLP/sentiment analysis?

3. What granularity is needed – individual-level trends for reviews, or team-level patterns for HRBP insights?

4. How should the agent handle biased language patterns in feedback (gendered descriptors, cultural bias)?

5. What privacy and consent requirements govern aggregation and analysis of peer feedback text?

▶ Annual/Semi-Annual Performance Review

A-305

Performance Review Narrative Assistant

L1: OOTB

Annual/Semi-Annual Performance Review

1. What is the review cadence, and how many manager-written reviews are generated per cycle?

2. Are there defined rating scale descriptions and behavioral anchors that narratives should align to?

3. What are the common quality issues (vague, biased, inconsistent, too brief, too generous)?

4. How will AI-generated drafts be positioned to managers – starting points for editing or near-final content?

5. What guardrails prevent legally risky language (implied promises, discriminatory phrasing)?

A-306

Calibration Analytics Agent

L4: Data Agent

Annual/Semi-Annual Performance Review

1. What historical calibration data exists (pre/post ratings, manager patterns, demographic distributions)?

2. What statistical methods should identify outliers (z-scores, deviation from norms, manager consistency)?

3. How should potential bias indicators be surfaced without creating legal discovery risk?

4. What access controls are needed – who sees pre-calibration distributions vs. final ratings?

5. How will outputs be delivered – live in calibration meetings, or as pre-read materials?

A-307

Review Cycle Orchestration Agent

L2: Agent Dsgn

Annual/Semi-Annual Performance Review

1. What performance system runs the review cycle, and does it support API-based workflow triggers?

2. What is the current on-time completion rate, and where are the biggest bottlenecks?

3. What escalation tiers exist for overdue submissions (reminder, manager nudge, VP escalation)?

4. What post-review actions should auto-trigger (compensation link, development plan, promotion workflow)?

5. How should exceptions be handled (employees on leave, new hires below tenure threshold, mid-cycle transfers)?

▶ Succession Planning

A-308

Successor Readiness Assessment Agent

L3: Custom ADK

Succession Planning

1. What data sources feed the 9-box today (performance ratings, potential assessments, 360 feedback, leadership programs)?

2. How are “potential” and “readiness” defined and measured – subjective judgment or structured assessment?

3. What balance between data-driven scoring and human judgment is desired in successor identification?

4. How will bias risk in potential assessments be addressed (demographic patterns in HiPo identification)?

5. What access controls and confidentiality requirements apply to succession data and 9-box placements?

A-309

Succession Pipeline Dashboard Agent

L4: Data Agent

Succession Planning

1. What constitutes a “critical role” and how many are currently tracked?

2. What bench strength ratio targets exist (e.g., 2 ready-now successors per critical role)?

3. What risk flags should the dashboard surface (single points of failure, retirement eligibility, successor attrition)?

4. What is the refresh cadence – real-time, quarterly for talent reviews, or annually for board reporting?

5. Who consumes the dashboard (CHRO, BU heads, Board), and what drill-down capability is needed?

▶ High-Potential (HiPo) Program Management

A-310

HiPo Identification & Nomination Agent

L3: Custom ADK

High-Potential (HiPo) Program Management

1. What are the current HiPo criteria, and how consistently are they applied across BUs and geographies?

2. What data sources feed assessment (performance history, 360 results, manager nominations, psychometrics)?

3. How will equity concerns in HiPo identification be addressed (underrepresentation of certain demographics)?

4. What is the selection committee structure, and how should AI-generated candidate profiles be presented?

5. What is the target HiPo pool size relative to total population, and how frequently is the pool refreshed?

A-311

HiPo Development Journey Agent

L2: Agent Dsgn

High-Potential (HiPo) Program Management

1. What development activities are available (stretch assignments, executive mentoring, cross-functional rotations)?

2. What systems track development journey progress (LMS, mentoring platform, project management, HRIS)?
