         -> Employee Engagement Surveys

Primary Personas: HRBP, People Analytics

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Design survey & launch communications

Survey questions designed manually; year-over-year comparability sometimes broken

Launch communications drafted individually per channel

Response rate monitoring is periodic

Survey Design & Communication Agent assists question design, generates launch communications emphasizing confidentiality across all channels

Survey design becomes more rigorous; launch communications are consistent and multi-channel; response rate monitoring becomes continuous

Survey response rate; time to design and launch survey; year-over-year question comparability rate

 

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Analyze results & drive action planning

Analysis done by People Analytics team (weeks to deliver)

Results shared in static PowerPoint decks

Action planning is unstructured; follow-through inconsistent

No connection between engagement and business outcomes

Engagement Insight Synthesizer Agent performs multi-dimensional analysis, identifies drivers, generates leadership reports and team-level action planning guides

Engagement-to-Outcome Correlation Agent joins engagement scores with business outcomes to identify which drivers have strongest causal impact; measures action plan effectiveness over time

Insights delivered in days instead of weeks; action plans become evidence-based and prioritized by business impact; follow-through tracked systematically

Insight delivery cycle time; action plan completion rate; engagement score trend; engagement-to-outcome correlation strength

 

 

-> Recognition & Rewards Programs

Primary Personas: HRBP, HR Ops Mgr

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Drive adoption & celebrate milestones

Recognition adoption varies widely by team and manager

Work anniversaries and milestones tracked via HRIS but celebrated inconsistently

Low-recognition managers not identified or coached

Recognition Nudge & Celebration Agent sends proactive nudges to low-recognition managers, auto-generates milestone celebrations, surfaces recognition opportunities

Recognition becomes more equitable across the organization; milestones celebrated consistently; manager recognition behavior improves through nudging

Recognition frequency by team/manager; recognition equity across demographics; milestone celebration coverage rate

 

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Analyze program effectiveness

Recognition data analyzed in spreadsheets periodically

No systematic correlation with engagement or retention

Budget utilization tracked manually

Recognition Program Analytics Agent analyzes frequency, distribution equity, budget utilization, and correlates recognition patterns with engagement and retention outcomes

Program effectiveness becomes measurable and linked to business outcomes; budget optimization becomes data-driven

Recognition-to-engagement correlation; recognition-to-retention correlation; budget utilization rate; recognition equity index

         -> Internal Communications & Culture Initiatives

Primary Personas: CHRO, HRBP, DEI Mgr

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Draft & distribute communications

Communications drafted manually per audience and format

Translation coordinated with vendors for global workforce

Content calendar managed in spreadsheets

Employee Communication Drafter drafts communications across formats with tone calibration, multi-audience variants, and translation support for global workforces

Communication drafting time significantly reduced; consistent messaging across audiences and languages; content calendar management streamlined

Communication drafting time; message consistency audit score; translation turnaround time

 

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Measure reach & sentiment

Communication analytics limited to email open rates

Sentiment analysis nonexistent or manual

No systematic feedback loop to improve content

Communication Reach & Sentiment Analyzer queries analytics data across channels to measure effectiveness and recommend optimization strategies

Communication effectiveness becomes measurable beyond open rates; sentiment tracking enables continuous content improvement

Communication reach by channel; employee sentiment score; content optimization actions per cycle; town hall attendance rate

 

 

Transforming HR Operations & Shared Services

 

As-Is Systems

To-Be Systems

Systems

HRIS, payroll system (ADP/Ceridian), time & attendance, HR service desk (ServiceNow), knowledge base (SharePoint/Confluence), IT ticketing, benefits carrier portals, email, spreadsheets (checklists, reconciliation, reports)

HRIS (via API), payroll (via API), time & attendance (via API), HR service desk (via API), knowledge base (searchable corpus), IT ticketing (via API), benefits carriers (via API), survey platform (via API), Gemini Enterprise, analytics warehouse

         -> Employee Data Management (HRIS)

Primary Personas: HR Ops Mgr, HRIS Analyst

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Process lifecycle transactions & maintain data

Lifecycle transactions (hires, promotions, transfers, terms) processed manually in HRIS

Data validation is post-hoc via periodic audits

Downstream system triggers (payroll, benefits, access) are manual

Employee Data Change Orchestrator processes transactions via HRIS APIs, validates inputs, applies business rules, triggers downstream integrations, maintains audit trails

Transaction processing becomes automated with built-in validation; downstream systems updated in real-time rather than batch; human effort shifts from data entry to exception handling

Transaction processing time; data entry error rate; downstream system sync lag; audit finding rate

 

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Monitor data quality & integration health

Data quality issues discovered during reporting or audits

Integration sync errors handled reactively

No systematic data quality scoring

HRIS Data Quality Monitor Agent runs automated quality checks (completeness, accuracy, timeliness), monitors integration sync health, flags exceptions, generates data quality scorecards

Data quality management becomes continuous and proactive; integration health monitored in real-time; quality trends tracked systematically

Data quality score by dimension; integration sync error rate; exception resolution time; data completeness rate

-> HR Service Delivery (Tier 0/1/2)

Primary Personas: HR Ops Mgr

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Resolve employee queries

Queries handled via email, phone, or portal; routed by category

Tier-0 self-service limited to static FAQ pages

Tier-1 agents search knowledge base manually; quality varies

Employee-specific data lookup requires HRIS access and manual search

Employee Query Resolution Agent searches knowledge base, looks up employee-specific data in HRIS (leave balance, benefits, pay stubs), resolves directly or routes with full context

Query resolution becomes instant for routine questions; employee-specific lookups automated; specialist time reserved for complex cases; 24/7 availability

First-contact resolution rate; average resolution time; Tier-0 deflection rate; employee CSAT score

 

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Monitor service delivery performance & improve

SLA adherence tracked via periodic report pulls

Knowledge base gaps identified anecdotally

No systematic continuous improvement process

Service Delivery Analytics Agent queries service desk data for volumes, resolution times, SLA adherence, CSAT, and identifies knowledge base gaps driving repeat queries

Service delivery insights become continuous; knowledge base gaps identified systematically; improvement priorities driven by data rather than anecdote

SLA adherence rate; knowledge base coverage vs. query topics; repeat query rate; CSAT trend

         -> Payroll Processing

Primary Personas: Payroll & Compliance Specialist

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Validate payroll inputs

Time & attendance exceptions reviewed manually

New hire/change/termination data cross-referenced between HRIS and payroll manually

Garnishment orders processed individually

Missing data discovered during payroll run (costly corrections)

Payroll Input Validation Agent cross-validates inputs across T&A, HRIS, and benefits systems; flags discrepancies (missing timesheets, unprocessed changes, garnishment conflicts); generates pre-payroll exception reports

Input validation becomes systematic and pre-run rather than discovered during processing; exception-based workflow replaces full manual review

Pre-run exception detection rate; payroll correction rate; processing error cost; input validation cycle time

 

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Post-run reconciliation & compliance

Register-to-GL reconciliation done manually in spreadsheets

Tax liability verification across jurisdictions is labor-intensive

Year-end processing (W-2) requires weeks of preparation

Audit documentation compiled manually

Payroll Reconciliation & Compliance Agent performs post-run reconciliation (register-to-GL, tax liability, benefit deductions), identifies variances, generates compliance-ready tax filing summaries and audit documentation

Post-run reconciliation becomes automated; multi-jurisdiction tax compliance verified systematically; year-end processing preparation dramatically compressed

Reconciliation variance detection rate; tax filing accuracy; year-end processing cycle time; audit readiness score

-> Offboarding & Exit Management

Primary Personas: HR Ops Mgr, HRBP

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Execute offboarding workflow

Exit triggers manual checklist across 5-6 systems

IT deprovisioning, asset recovery, final pay calculation each require separate manual steps

COBRA/benefits notices sent manually with compliance deadlines

Day-of-departure readiness failures common (access not revoked)

Offboarding Orchestration Agent triggers all offboarding workflows upon separation: IT deprovisioning tickets, asset recovery, final pay, COBRA notices; tracks completion with escalation for overdue items

Offboarding becomes fully orchestrated rather than manually coordinated; compliance deadlines met systematically; security risk from delayed access revocation eliminated

Time from notification to full deprovisioning; offboarding task completion rate by deadline; COBRA notice compliance rate; security audit finding rate on access revocation

 

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Analyze exit interviews & attrition patterns

Exit interviews completed inconsistently; analyzed manually if at all

Attrition analysis limited to basic turnover rate reporting

No predictive early-warning capability

Connection between exit themes and engagement data not made

Exit Interview Insight Synthesizer analyzes batch transcripts for themes, sentiment, and actionable recommendations by department, manager, tenure band

Attrition Analytics Agent queries separation data for attrition patterns, correlates with engagement, and generates predictive early-warning indicators

Exit insights become systematic and actionable rather than anecdotal; attrition patterns analyzed multi-dimensionally; predictive capabilities enable proactive intervention

Exit interview completion rate; insight-to-action conversion rate; regrettable attrition rate; predictive early-warning accuracy

 Domain 9: DEI & Belonging

 

As-Is Systems

To-Be Systems

Systems

HRIS, ATS, survey platform, compensation system, event management tools, spreadsheets (DEI dashboards, ERG tracking, EEO-1 reports), PowerPoint (board presentations), external reporting portals

HRIS (via API), ATS (via API), survey platform (via API), compensation system (via API), event platforms (via API), Gemini Enterprise, analytics warehouse

         -> DEI Strategy & Metrics

Primary Personas: DEI Mgr, CHRO

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Track representation & report metrics

Representation data pulled manually from HRIS into Excel

Dashboard creation is manual and labor-intensive

External reporting (EEO-1, DEI disclosures) compiled manually

Small group data suppression applied inconsistently

DEI Dashboard & Reporting Agent queries workforce demographics, hiring funnel diversity, promotion equity, inclusion index, and pay-gap data; generates dashboards, board reports, and external submissions with consistent data suppression rules

DEI visibility becomes real-time and comprehensive; reporting automation eliminates manual compilation; consistent methodology across all reporting audiences

Representation metrics by level/function; DEI report generation time; external reporting accuracy; data suppression compliance rate

 

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Audit inclusive hiring & drive programs

Hiring funnel diversity analyzed periodically (if at all)

Drop-off points in the funnel unidentified

DEI programming proposals drafted manually

Executive sponsorship materials created ad-hoc

Inclusive Hiring Audit Agent analyzes hiring funnel at each stage by demographic dimensions; identifies drop-off points; recommends targeted interventions

DEI Communication & Programming Assistant generates strategy narratives, initiative proposals, executive sponsorship briefings, and cultural programming content

Hiring funnel equity gaps identified systematically with targeted interventions; DEI programming becomes strategically planned with executive-ready justifications

Hiring funnel conversion rate by demographic dimension; diversity drop-off points identified; DEI initiative proposal-to-approval rate; program participation rate

-> ERG (Employee Resource Group) Management

Primary Personas: DEI Mgr

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Track engagement, measure impact & report

ERG membership and event data tracked in spreadsheets

Business impact of ERGs is anecdotal

Budget utilization tracked manually

Executive sponsor reporting is ad-hoc

ERG Engagement & Impact Agent connects to event platforms and HRIS to track membership, attendance, budget utilization; generates impact reports linking ERG participation to retention and engagement for executive sponsors

ERG impact becomes measurable and linked to business outcomes; executive sponsorship justified with data rather than stories; budget allocation becomes evidence-based

ERG membership engagement rate; event attendance trend; ERG participation-to-retention correlation; budget utilization rate; executive sponsor satisfaction

Transforming People Analytics & HR Technology

 

As-Is Systems

To-Be Systems

Systems

HR data warehouse/lake (BigQuery/Snowflake/Redshift), HRIS, ATS, LMS, survey platform, BI tools (Looker/Tableau/Power BI), IT asset management, vendor management systems, spreadsheets (ad-hoc analysis, vendor evaluations), email

HR data warehouse (via SQL/API), HRIS (via API), ATS (via API), LMS (via API), survey platform (via API), IT asset management (via API), Gemini Enterprise, Vertex AI (ML serving)

         -> Workforce Analytics & Reporting

Primary Personas: People Analytics Analyst, HRBP, CHRO

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Build dashboards & handle ad-hoc analysis

Dashboards built by People Analytics team in BI tools (backlog of requests)

Ad-hoc analysis requests take days-to-weeks depending on complexity

Self-service analytics is limited to pre-built reports

Complex joins across HRIS, ATS, LMS, survey data require technical SQL skills

HR Data Lake Query Agent enables natural-language queries across the unified HR data lake; translates questions into SQL, executes, and presents results with visualizations

Self-service analytics available to HRBPs and leaders without SQL skills

Workforce Cost Modeling Agent models fully-loaded workforce costs under different scenarios for executive decision-making

Ad-hoc analysis becomes self-service and instant; People Analytics team shifts from report production to strategic insight generation; cost modeling becomes on-demand

Ad-hoc analysis turnaround time; self-service query volume; People Analytics team capacity for strategic projects; report request backlog

 

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Predictive modeling & intervention

Predictive models (attrition, performance) built manually by data scientists (if at all)

Flight-risk identification is anecdotal or manager-dependent

Intervention workflows are manual and inconsistent

Attrition Prediction & Intervention Agent runs ML models on HRIS, engagement, and manager data to generate flight-risk scores; triggers HRBP alerts, suggests retention actions, drafts stay-interview talking points

Attrition prediction becomes systematic and continuous; intervention workflows are proactive and orchestrated rather than reactive and ad-hoc

Flight-risk prediction accuracy; intervention trigger rate; retention rate of flagged employees post-intervention; model retraining frequency

-> HR Technology Governance

Primary Personas: HRIS Analyst

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Manage tech stack & optimize licenses

HR tech inventory maintained in spreadsheets or IT asset system

License utilization unmonitored; waste undetected

Contract renewals tracked via calendar reminders

Cost optimization opportunities identified ad-hoc

HR Tech Stack Intelligence Agent maintains real-time tech inventory, tracks license utilization, flags underused tools, generates renewal timeline alerts with cost optimization recommendations

Tech stack visibility becomes continuous; license waste identified proactively; renewal preparedness improves; cost optimization becomes systematic

License utilization rate; tech stack cost trend; renewal preparedness (days before deadline); cost optimization savings identified

 

Activity Step

As-Is State

To-Be State (with Agents)

Expected Qualitative Impact

Key KPI Metrics

Evaluate & select new vendors

Vendor evaluation process ad-hoc; inconsistent scoring

Demo notes captured in scattered documents

Comparison across vendors requires manual synthesis

Decision-making process informal

Vendor Evaluation Assistant structures RFP documents, generates evaluation scorecards from demo notes, creates comparative analysis summaries across vendor options

Vendor evaluation becomes structured and consistent; comparison becomes objective and documented; decision-making becomes transparent and auditable

Vendor evaluation cycle time; scoring methodology consistency; stakeholder alignment rate on vendor selection; post-implementation satisfaction vs. evaluation score
